  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /***************************************************************************\\n2:  *                                  _   _ ____  _\\n3:  *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\\\| |\\n4:  *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\\n5:  *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\\n6:  *                             \\\\___|\\\\___/|_| \\\\_\\\\_____|\\n7:  *\\n8:  * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\\n9:  *\\n10:  * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\\n11:  * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\\n12:  * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\\n13:  *\\n14:  * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\\n15:  * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\\n16:  * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\\n17:  *\\n18:  * This software is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\\n19:  * KIND, either express or implied.\\n20:  *\\n21:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\\n22:  *\\n23:  ***************************************************************************/\\n24: \\n25: #include \\\"curl_setup.h\\\"\\n26: \\n27: #include <limits.h>\\n28: \\n29: #ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\\n30: #include <netinet/in.h>\\n31: #endif\\n32: \\n33: #ifdef HAVE_LINUX_TCP_H\\n34: #include <linux/tcp.h>\\n35: #elif defined(HAVE_NETINET_TCP_H)\\n36: #include <netinet/tcp.h>\\n37: #endif\\n38: \\n39: #include \\\"urldata.h\\\"\\n40: #include \\\"url.h\\\"\\n41: #include \\\"progress.h\\\"\\n42: #include \\\"content_encoding.h\\\"\\n43: #include \\\"strcase.h\\\"\\n44: #include \\\"share.h\\\"\\n45: #include \\\"vtls/vtls.h\\\"\\n46: #include \\\"warnless.h\\\"\\n47: #include \\\"sendf.h\\\"\\n48: #include \\\"http2.h\\\"\\n49: #include \\\"setopt.h\\\"\\n50: #include \\\"multiif.h\\\"\\n51: #include \\\"altsvc.h\\\"\\n52: #include \\\"hsts.h\\\"\\n53: \\n54: /* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\\n55: #include \\\"curl_printf.h\\\"\\n56: #include \\\"curl_memory.h\\\"\\n57: #include \\\"memdebug.h\\\"\\n58: \\n59: CURLcode Curl_setstropt(char **charp, const char *s)\\n60: {\\n61:   /* Release the previous storage at `charp' and replace by a dynamic storage\\n62:      copy of `s'. Return CURLE_OK or CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY. */\\n63: \\n64:   Curl_safefree(*charp);\\n65: \\n66:   if(s) {\\n67:     if(strlen(s) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\\n68:       return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\\n69: \\n70:     *charp = strdup(s);\\n71:     if(!*charp)\\n72:       return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\\n73:   }\\n74: \\n75:   return CURLE_OK;\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: CURLcode Curl_setblobopt(struct curl_blob **blobp,\\n79:                          const struct curl_blob *blob)\\n80: {\\n81:   /* free the previous storage at `blobp' and replace by a dynamic storage\\n82:      copy of blob. If CURL_BLOB_COPY is set, the data is copied. */\\n83: \\n84:   Curl_safefree(*blobp);\\n85: \\n86:   if(blob) {\\n87:     struct curl_blob *nblob;\\n88:     if(blob->len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\\n89:       return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\\n90:     nblob = (struct curl_blob *)\\n91:       malloc(sizeof(struct curl_blob) +\\n92:              ((blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) ? blob->len : 0));\\n93:     if(!nblob)\\n94:       return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\\n95:     *nblob = *blob;\\n96:     if(blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) {\\n97:       /* put the data after the blob struct in memory */\\n98:       nblob->data = (char *)nblob + sizeof(struct curl_blob);\\n99:       memcpy(nblob->data, blob->data, blob->len);\\n100:     }\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/setopt.c%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /***************************************************************************\n2:  *                                  _   _ ____  _\n3:  *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n4:  *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n5:  *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n6:  *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n7:  *\n8:  * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n9:  *\n10:  * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n11:  * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n12:  * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n13:  *\n14:  * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n15:  * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n16:  * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n17:  *\n18:  * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n19:  * KIND, either express or implied.\n20:  *\n21:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n22:  *\n23:  ***************************************************************************/\n24: \n25: #include \"curl_setup.h\"\n26: \n27: #include <limits.h>\n28: \n29: #ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\n30: #include <netinet/in.h>\n31: #endif\n32: \n33: #ifdef HAVE_LINUX_TCP_H\n34: #include <linux/tcp.h>\n35: #elif defined(HAVE_NETINET_TCP_H)\n36: #include <netinet/tcp.h>\n37: #endif\n38: \n39: #include \"urldata.h\"\n40: #include \"url.h\"\n41: #include \"progress.h\"\n42: #include \"content_encoding.h\"\n43: #include \"strcase.h\"\n44: #include \"share.h\"\n45: #include \"vtls/vtls.h\"\n46: #include \"warnless.h\"\n47: #include \"sendf.h\"\n48: #include \"http2.h\"\n49: #include \"setopt.h\"\n50: #include \"multiif.h\"\n51: #include \"altsvc.h\"\n52: #include \"hsts.h\"\n53: \n54: /* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\n55: #include \"curl_printf.h\"\n56: #include \"curl_memory.h\"\n57: #include \"memdebug.h\"\n58: \n59: CURLcode Curl_setstropt(char **charp, const char *s)\n60: {\n61:   /* Release the previous storage at `charp' and replace by a dynamic storage\n62:      copy of `s'. Return CURLE_OK or CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY. */\n63: \n64:   Curl_safefree(*charp);\n65: \n66:   if(s) {\n67:     if(strlen(s) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n68:       return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n69: \n70:     *charp = strdup(s);\n71:     if(!*charp)\n72:       return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n73:   }\n74: \n75:   return CURLE_OK;\n76: }\n77: \n78: CURLcode Curl_setblobopt(struct curl_blob **blobp,\n79:                          const struct curl_blob *blob)\n80: {\n81:   /* free the previous storage at `blobp' and replace by a dynamic storage\n82:      copy of blob. If CURL_BLOB_COPY is set, the data is copied. */\n83: \n84:   Curl_safefree(*blobp);\n85: \n86:   if(blob) {\n87:     struct curl_blob *nblob;\n88:     if(blob->len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n89:       return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n90:     nblob = (struct curl_blob *)\n91:       malloc(sizeof(struct curl_blob) +\n92:              ((blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) ? blob->len : 0));\n93:     if(!nblob)\n94:       return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n95:     *nblob = *blob;\n96:     if(blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) {\n97:       /* put the data after the blob struct in memory */\n98:       nblob->data = (char *)nblob + sizeof(struct curl_blob);\n99:       memcpy(nblob->data, blob->data, blob->len);\n100:     }\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/setopt.c%3FL1-100"
  }
}

27. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 1280,
  "path": "lib/urldata.h",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d",
  "startLine": 1239
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1239: /**\\n1240:  * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others\\n1241:  * on the same connection.\\n1242:  * TODO: we need to adapt it to the new priority scheme as defined in RFC 9218\\n1243:  */\\n1244: struct Curl_data_priority {\\n1245: #ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\\n1246:   /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\\n1247:   struct Curl_easy *parent;\\n1248:   struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\\n1249: #endif\\n1250:   int weight;\\n1251: #ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\\n1252:   BIT(exclusive);\\n1253: #endif\\n1254: };\\n1255: \\n1256: /*\\n1257:  * This struct is for holding data that was attempted to get sent to the user's\\n1258:  * callback but is held due to pausing. One instance per type (BOTH, HEADER,\\n1259:  * BODY).\\n1260:  */\\n1261: struct tempbuf {\\n1262:   struct dynbuf b;\\n1263:   int type;   /* type of the 'tempwrite' buffer as a bitmask that is used with\\n1264:                  Curl_client_write() */\\n1265:   BIT(paused_body); /* if PAUSE happened before/during BODY write */\\n1266: };\\n1267: \\n1268: /* Timers */\\n1269: typedef enum {\\n1270:   EXPIRE_100_TIMEOUT,\\n1271:   EXPIRE_ASYNC_NAME,\\n1272:   EXPIRE_CONNECTTIMEOUT,\\n1273:   EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME, /* family1 */\\n1274:   EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME2, /* family2 */\\n1275:   EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_DNS, /* See asyn-ares.c */\\n1276:   EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS,\\n1277:   EXPIRE_MULTI_PENDING,\\n1278:   EXPIRE_RUN_NOW,\\n1279:   EXPIRE_SPEEDCHECK,\\n1280:   EXPIRE_TIMEOUT,\\n1281: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/urldata.h%3FL1239-1280\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1239: /**\n1240:  * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others\n1241:  * on the same connection.\n1242:  * TODO: we need to adapt it to the new priority scheme as defined in RFC 9218\n1243:  */\n1244: struct Curl_data_priority {\n1245: #ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n1246:   /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n1247:   struct Curl_easy *parent;\n1248:   struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n1249: #endif\n1250:   int weight;\n1251: #ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n1252:   BIT(exclusive);\n1253: #endif\n1254: };\n1255: \n1256: /*\n1257:  * This struct is for holding data that was attempted to get sent to the user's\n1258:  * callback but is held due to pausing. One instance per type (BOTH, HEADER,\n1259:  * BODY).\n1260:  */\n1261: struct tempbuf {\n1262:   struct dynbuf b;\n1263:   int type;   /* type of the 'tempwrite' buffer as a bitmask that is used with\n1264:                  Curl_client_write() */\n1265:   BIT(paused_body); /* if PAUSE happened before/during BODY write */\n1266: };\n1267: \n1268: /* Timers */\n1269: typedef enum {\n1270:   EXPIRE_100_TIMEOUT,\n1271:   EXPIRE_ASYNC_NAME,\n1272:   EXPIRE_CONNECTTIMEOUT,\n1273:   EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME, /* family1 */\n1274:   EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME2, /* family2 */\n1275:   EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_DNS, /* See asyn-ares.c */\n1276:   EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS,\n1277:   EXPIRE_MULTI_PENDING,\n1278:   EXPIRE_RUN_NOW,\n1279:   EXPIRE_SPEEDCHECK,\n1280:   EXPIRE_TIMEOUT,\n1281: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/urldata.h%3FL1239-1280"
  }
}

   /* how many iterations this single URL has with ranges\\n43:                            etc */\\n44:   curl_off_t li;\\n45: };\\n46: \\n47: struct OperationConfig {\\n48:   bool remote_time;\\n49:   char *useragent;\\n50:   struct curl_slist *cookies;  /* cookies to serialize into a single line */\\n51:   char *cookiejar;          /* write to this file */\\n52:   struct curl_slist *cookiefiles;  /* file(s) to load cookies from */\\n53:   char *altsvc;             /* alt-svc cache file name */\\n54:   char *hsts;               /* HSTS cache file name */\\n55:   bool cookiesession;       /* new session? */\\n56:   bool encoding;            /* Accept-Encoding please */\\n57:   bool tr_encoding;         /* Transfer-Encoding please */\\n58:   unsigned long authtype;   /* auth bitmask */\\n59:   bool use_resume;\\n60:   bool resume_from_current;\\n61:   bool disable_epsv;\\n62:   bool disable_eprt;\\n63:   bool ftp_pret;\\n64:   char *proto_str;\\n65:   bool proto_present;\\n66:   char *proto_redir_str;\\n67:   bool proto_redir_present;\\n68:   char *proto_default;\\n69:   curl_off_t resume_from;\\n70:   char *postfields;\\n71:   curl_off_t postfieldsize;\\n72:   char *referer;\\n73:   char *query;\\n74:   long timeout_ms;\\n75:   long connecttimeout_ms;\\n76:   long maxredirs;\\n77:   curl_off_t max_filesize;\\n78:   char *output_dir;\\n79:   char *headerfile;\\n80:   char *ftpport;\\n81:   char *iface;\\n82:   long localport;\\n83:   long localportrange;\\n84:   unsigned short porttouse;\\n85:   char *range;\\n86:   long low_speed_limit;\\n87:   long low_speed_time;\\n88:   char *dns_servers;   /* dot notation: 1.1.1.1;2.2.2.2 */\\n89:   char *dns_interface; /* interface name */\\n90:   char *dns_ipv4_addr; /* dot notation */\\n91:   char *dns_ipv6_addr; /* dot notation */\\n92:   char *userpwd;\\n93:   char *login_options;\\n94:   char *tls_username;\\n95:   char *tls_password;\\n96:   char *tls_authtype;\\n97:   char *proxy_tls_username;\\n98:   char *proxy_tls_password;\\n99:   char *proxy_tls_authtype;\\n100:   char *proxyuserpwd;\\n101:   char *proxy;\\n102:   int proxyver;             /* set to CURLPROXY_HTTP* define */\\n103:   char *noproxy;\\n104:   char *mail_from;\\n105:   struct curl_slist *mail_rcpt;\\n106:   char *mail_auth;\\n107:   bool mail_rcpt_allowfails; /* --mail-rcpt-allowfails */\\n108:   char *sasl_authzid;       /* Authorization identity (identity to use) */\\n109:   bool sasl_ir;             /* Enable/disable SASL initial response */\\n110:   bool proxytunnel;\\n111:   bool ftp_append;          /* APPE on ftp */\\n112:   bool use_ascii;           /* select ascii or text transfer */\\n113:   bool autoreferer;         /* automatically set referer */\\n114:   bool failonerror;         /* fail on (HTTP) errors */\\n115:   bool failwithbody;        /* fail on (HTTP) errors but still store body */\\n116:   bool show_headers;        /* show headers to data output */\\n117:   bool no_body;             /* don't get the body */\\n118:   bool dirlistonly;         /* only get the FTP dir list */\\n119:   bool followlocation;      /* follow http redirects */\\n120:   bool unrestricted_auth;   /* Continue to send authentication (user+password)\\n121:                                when following redirects, even when hostname\\n122:                                changed */\\n123:   bool netrc_opt;\\n124:   bool netrc;\\n125:   char *netrc_file;\\n126:   struct getout *url_list;   /* point to the node to fill in outfile */\\n130:   struct getout *url_ul;    /* point to the node to fill in upload */\\n131:   char *ipfs_gateway;\\n132:   char *doh_url;\\n133:   char *cipher_list;\\n134:   char *proxy_cipher_list;\\n135:   char *cipher13_list;\\n136:   char *proxy_cipher13_list;\\n137:   char *cert;\\n138:   char *proxy_cert;\\n139:   char *cert_type;\\n140:   char *proxy_cert_type;\\n141:   char *cacert;\\n142:   char *proxy_cacert;\\n143:   char *capath;\\n144:   char *proxy_capath;\\n145:   char *crlfile;\\n146:   char *proxy_crlfile;\\n147:   char *pinnedpubkey;\\n148:   char *proxy_pinnedpubkey;\\n149:   char *key;\\n150:   char *proxy_key;\\n151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/src/tool_cfgable.h%3FL40-150\"}",

    "content": "40:   curl_off_t infilenum; /* number of files to upload */\n41:   curl_off_t up;        /* upload file counter within a single upload glob */\n42:   curl_off_t urlnum;    /* how many iterations this single URL has with ranges\n43:                            etc */\n44:   curl_off_t li;\n45: };\n46: \n47: struct OperationConfig {\n48:   bool remote_time;\n49:   char *useragent;\n50:   struct curl_slist *cookies;  /* cookies to serialize into a single line */\n51:   char *cookiejar;          /* write to this file */\n52:   struct curl_slist *cookiefiles;  /* file(s) to load cookies from */\n53:   char *altsvc;             /* alt-svc cache file name */\n54:   char *hsts;               /* HSTS cache file name */\n55:   bool cookiesession;       /* new session? */\n56:   bool encoding;            /* Accept-Encoding please */\n57:   bool tr_encoding;         /* Transfer-Encoding please */\n58:   unsigned long authtype;   /* auth bitmask */\n59:   bool use_resume;\n60:   bool resume_from_current;\n61:   bool disable_epsv;\n62:   bool disable_eprt;\n63:   bool ftp_pret;\n64:   char *proto_str;\n65:   bool proto_present;\n66:   char *proto_redir_str;\n67:   bool proto_redir_present;\n68:   char *proto_default;\n69:   curl_off_t resume_from;\n70:   char *postfields;\n71:   curl_off_t postfieldsize;\n72:   char *referer;\n73:   char *query;\n74:   long timeout_ms;\n75:   long connecttimeout_ms;\n76:   long maxredirs;\n77:   curl_off_t max_filesize;\n78:   char *output_dir;\n79:   char *headerfile;\n80:   char *ftpport;\n81:   char *iface;\n82:   long localport;\n83:   long localportrange;\n84:   unsigned short porttouse;\n85:   char *range;\n86:   long low_speed_limit;\n87:   long low_speed_time;\n88:   char *dns_servers;   /* dot notation: 1.1.1.1;2.2.2.2 */\n89:   char *dns_interface; /* interface name */\n90:   char *dns_ipv4_addr; /* dot notation */\n91:   char *dns_ipv6_addr; /* dot notation */\n92:   char *userpwd;\n93:   char *login_options;\n94:   char *tls_username;\n95:   char *tls_password;\n96:   char *tls_authtype;\n97:   char *proxy_tls_username;\n98:   char *proxy_tls_password;\n99:   char *proxy_tls_authtype;\n100:   char *proxyuserpwd;\n101:   char *proxy;\n102:   int proxyver;             /* set to CURLPROXY_HTTP* define */\n103:   char *noproxy;\n104:   char *mail_from;\n105:   struct curl_slist *mail_rcpt;\n106:   char *mail_auth;\n107:   bool mail_rcpt_allowfails; /* --mail-rcpt-allowfails */\n108:   char *sasl_authzid;       /* Authorization identity (identity to use) */\n109:   bool sasl_ir;             /* Enable/disable SASL initial response */\n110:   bool proxytunnel;\n111:   bool ftp_append;          /* APPE on ftp */\n112:   bool use_ascii;           /* select ascii or text transfer */\n113:   bool autoreferer;         /* automatically set referer */\n114:   bool failonerror;         /* fail on (HTTP) errors */\n115:   bool failwithbody;        /* fail on (HTTP) errors but still store body */\n116:   bool show_headers;        /* show headers to data output */\n117:   bool no_body;             /* don't get the body */\n118:   bool dirlistonly;         /* only get the FTP dir list */\n119:   bool followlocation;      /* follow http redirects */\n120:   bool unrestricted_auth;   /* Continue to send authentication (user+password)\n121:                                when following redirects, even when hostname\n122:                                changed */\n123:   bool netrc_opt;\n124:   bool netrc;\n125:   char *netrc_file;\n126:   struct getout *url_list;   /* point to the node to fill in outfile */\n130:   struct getout *url_ul;    /* point to the node to fill in upload */\n131:   char *ipfs_gateway;\n132:   char *doh_url;\n133:   char *cipher_list;\n134:   char *proxy_cipher_list;\n135:   char *cipher13_list;\n136:   char *proxy_cipher13_list;\n137:   char *cert;\n138:   char *proxy_cert;\n139:   char *cert_type;\n140:   char *proxy_cert_type;\n141:   char *cacert;\n142:   char *proxy_cacert;\n143:   char *capath;\n144:   char *proxy_capath;\n145:   char *crlfile;\n146:   char *proxy_crlfile;\n147:   char *pinnedpubkey;\n148:   char *proxy_pinnedpubkey;\n149:   char *key;\n150:   char *proxy_key;\n151: ",

}

            "content": "61: \n62:   curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES, list);\n63:   curl_easy_perform(curl);\n64: ",

          },
          {
            "content": "24: .\\\"\n25: .TH CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES 3 \"17 Jun 2014\" libcurl libcurl\n26: .SH NAME\n27: CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES \\- alternative matches for HTTP 200 OK\n28: .SH SYNOPSIS\n29: ",
            "endLine": 29,
            "startLine": 24
          },
          {
            "content": "31: \n32: CURLcode curl_easy_setopt(CURL *handle, CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES,\n33:                           struct curl_slist *aliases);\n34: .SH DESCRIPTION\n35: Pass a pointer to a linked list of \\fIaliases\\fP to be treated as valid HTTP\n36: 200 responses.  Some servers respond with a custom header response line.  For\n37: ",
            "endLine": 37,
            "startLine": 31
          }
        ],

41. Write @ -
Input
{

Input
{
  "command": "cat > fetch_files.py << 'EOF'\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Fetch curl source files from Sourcegraph for modification\"\"\"\nimport subprocess\nimport json\n\nfiles_to_fetch = [\n    (\"include/curl/curl.h\", \"include/curl\"),\n    (\"lib/urldata.h\", \"lib\"),\n    (\"lib/setopt.c\", \"lib\"),\n    (\"src/tool_cfgable.h\", \"src\"),\n    (\"src/tool_getparam.c\", \"src\"),\n    (\"src/tool_operate.c\", \"src\"),\n]\n\nrepo = \"github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d\"\n\nfor file_path, dir_path in files_to_fetch:\n    print(f\"Creating directory {dir_path}...\")\n    subprocess.run([\"mkdir\", \"-p\", dir_path], check=True)\n    \nprint(\"\\nNote: Files would be fetched from Sourcegraph API\")\nprint(\"Using sg_read_file tool instead for large files\")\nEOF\npython3 fetch_files.py\n",
  "description": "Create a placeholder script for file fetching"
}

      "text": "Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information about HTTP/2 priority implementation in the curl repository. Let me compile the final report.\n\n## Summary: HTTP/2 Priority Implementation References in curl\n\nI have completed a comprehensive search of the repository `github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d` for all references to HTTP/2 priority implementation. Based on the TODO comment at `/workspace/lib/urldata.h:1242`, I've identified all the files and line ranges where priorities are mentioned.\n\n### Key Files and Line Ranges\n\n#### Core Data Structures:\n1. **lib/urldata.h:1232-1254** - Priority structure definitions\n   - `struct Curl_data_prio_node` (lines 1233-1236)\n   - `struct Curl_data_priority` (lines 1244-1254)\n   - TODO comment about RFC 9218 adaptation (line 1242)\n\n2. **lib/urldata.h:1400-1402** - State priority field in `struct Curl_easy`\n\n3. **lib/urldata.h:1811-1813** - Set priority field in `struct Curl_easy`\n\n#### API Options (curl_easy_setopt):\n4. **include/curl/curl.h:1950-1958** - CURL option definitions\n   - `CURLOPT_STREAM_WEIGHT` (line 1951)\n   - `CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS` (line 1954)\n   - `CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E` (line 1957)\n\n5. **lib/setopt.c:2995-3013** - Option handling\n   - `CURLOPT_STREAM_WEIGHT` handling (lines 2995-3003)\n   - `CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS` and `CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E` handling (lines 3004-3012)\n\n6. **lib/easyoptions.c:321-323** - Easy options mapping\n\n#### HTTP/2 Priority Implementation:\n7. **lib/http2.c:1753-1810** - Priority weight and spec functions\n   - `sweight_wanted()` (lines 1753-1758)\n   - `sweight_in_effect()` (lines 1760-1765)\n   - `h2_pri_spec()` (lines 1773-1783)\n   - `h2_progress_egress()` - priority update logic (lines 1791-1810)\n\n8. **lib/http2.c:782-784** - Duplicate handle priority setup\n\n9. **lib/http2.c:1115-1118** - NGHTTP2_PRIORITY frame handling\n\n10. **lib/cf-h2-proxy.c:900-920** - HTTP/2 proxy submit with priority spec\n\n#### Priority Tree Management:\n11. **lib/url.c:3990-4090** - Priority tree operations\n    - `priority_remove_child()` (lines 3990-4010)\n    - `Curl_data_priority_add_child()` (lines 4012-4059)\n    - `data_priority_cleanup()` (lines 4064-4075)\n\n12. **lib/url.c:4078-4081** - `Curl_data_priority_clear_state()`\n\n#### Function Declarations:\n13. **lib/url.h:62-74** - Function declarations for priority operations\n\n14. **lib/url.h (forward declarations)** - Conditional declarations based on HTTP/2/HTTP/3\n\n#### Transfer and Cleanup:\n15. **lib/transfer.c:1376** - Priority state clearing during transfer setup\n\n#### Test Files:\n16. **tests/libtest/lib2404.c:89-91** - Stream weight test case\n\n17. **tests/data/test2404:60-62** - HTTP/2 STREAM_WEIGHT test\n\n#### Documentation:\n18. **docs/libcurl/opts/CURLOPT_STREAM_WEIGHT.3:25-63** - Documentation\n19. **docs/libcurl/opts/CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS.3:25-63** - Documentation\n20. **docs/libcurl/opts/CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E.3:25-68** - Documentation\n21. **docs/libcurl/curl_easy_setopt.3:382-390** - Option documentation\n22. **docs/libcurl/symbols-in-versions:852-856** - Symbol versions\n\n#### Build System:\n23. **packages/OS400/curl.inc.in:1490-1497** - Platform-specific includes\n\n### Implementation Details\n\n**Current HTTP/2 Priority Implementation (RFC 7540):**\n- Weight-based prioritization: 1-256 (default 16)\n- Tree-like dependencies with parent-child relationships\n- Exclusive flag support for moving siblings under dependent\n- nghttp2 backend handles actual PRIORITY frame transmission\n\n**HTTP/3 Support Status:**\n- Priority structure is defined for HTTP/3 (`USE_HTTP3` flag)\n- Currently only weight field is portable across HTTP/2 and HTTP/3\n- Tree dependencies and exclusive flag are nghttp2-only (#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2)\n- No HTTP/3-specific priority implementation found (RFC 9218 Extensible Priorities not yet implemented)\n\n### Files That Need RFC 9218 Updates\n\nFor HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) implementation, you'll need to modify:\n1. `/workspace/lib/urldata.h` - Extend `Curl_data_priority` struct\n2. `/workspace/lib/http2.c` - Add HTTP/3 priority handling\n3. `/workspace/lib/url.c` - Extend priority management functions\n4. `/workspace/lib/setopt.c` - Add new CURL options for extensible priorities\n5. `/workspace/include/curl/curl.h` - Add new option constants\n6. HTTP/3 backend files (vquic implementations) - Add priority support",

"{\"content\":\"1752: \\n1753: static int sweight_wanted(const struct Curl_easy *data)\\n1754: {\\n1755:   /* 0 weight is not set by user and we take the nghttp2 default one */\\n1756:   return data->set.priority.weight?\\n1757:     data->set.priority.weight : NGHTTP2_DEFAULT_WEIGHT;\\n1758: }\\n1759: \\n1760: static int sweight_in_effect(const struct Curl_easy *data)\\n1761: {\\n1762:   /* 0 weight is not set by user and we take the nghttp2 default one */\\n1763:   return data->state.priority.weight?\\n1764:     data->state.priority.weight : NGHTTP2_DEFAULT_WEIGHT;\\n1765: }\\n1766: \\n1767: /*\\n1768:  * h2_pri_spec() fills in the pri_spec struct, used by nghttp2 to send weight\\n1769:  * and dependency to the peer. It also stores the updated values in the state\\n1770:  * struct.\\n1771:  */\\n1772: \\n1773: static void h2_pri_spec(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n1774:                         nghttp2_priority_spec *pri_spec)\\n1775: {\\n1776:   struct Curl_data_priority *prio = &data->set.priority;\\n1777:   struct stream_ctx *depstream = H2_STREAM_CTX(prio->parent);\\n1778:   int32_t depstream_id = depstream? depstream->id:0;\\n1779:   nghttp2_priority_spec_init(pri_spec, depstream_id,\\n1780:                              sweight_wanted(data),\\n1781:                              data->set.priority.exclusive);\\n1782:   data->state.priority = *prio;\\n1783: }\\n1784: \\n1785: /*\\n1786:  * Check if there's been an update in the priority /\\n1787:  * dependency settings and if so it submits a PRIORITY frame with the updated\\n1788:  * info.\\n1789:  * Flush any out data pending in the network buffer.\\n1790:  */\\n1791: static CURLcode h2_progress_egress(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\\n1792:                                   struct Curl_easy *data)\\n1793: {\\n1794:   struct cf_h2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\\n1795:   struct stream_ctx *stream = H2_STREAM_CTX(data);\\n1796:   int rv = 0;\\n1797: \\n1798:   if(stream && stream->id > 0 &&\\n1799:      ((sweight_wanted(data) != sweight_in_effect(data)) ||\\n1800:       (data->set.priority.exclusive != data->state.priority.exclusive) ||\\n1801:       (data->set.priority.parent != data->state.priority.parent)) ) {\\n1802:     /* send new weight and/or dependency */\\n1803:     nghttp2_priority_spec pri_spec;\\n1804: \\n1805:     h2_pri_spec(data, &pri_spec);\\n1806:     CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \\\"[%d] Queuing PRIORITY\\\", stream->id);\\n1807:     DEBUGASSERT(stream->id != -1);\\n1808:     rv = nghttp2_submit_priority(ctx->h2, NGHTTP2_FLAG_NONE,\\n1809:                                  stream->id, &pri_spec);\\n1810:     if(rv)\\n1811: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/http2.c%3FL1752-1810\"}"

In contrast to HTTP/2 which can stop reading\\n662:            from TCP connection, HTTP/3 over QUIC needs ACK from server\\n663:            to ensure stream closure.  It should keep reading. */\\n664:         if(!is_http3) {\\n665:           k->keepon &= ~KEEP_RECV; /* we're done reading */\\n666: \",\"endLine\":666,\"startLine\":659},{\"content\":\"73: #include \\\"vtls/vtls.h\\\"\\n74: #include \\\"vquic/vquic.h\\\"\\n75: #include \\\"select.h\\\"\\n76: \",\"endLine\":76,\"startLine\":73},{\"content\":\"448:        incoming bytes for a particular stream. */\\n449:     bool is_http3 = Curl_conn_is_http3(data, conn, FIRSTSOCKET);\\n450:     data_eof_handled = is_http3 || Curl_conn_is_http2(data, conn, FIRSTSOCKET);\\n451: \\n452: \",\"endLine\":452,\"startLine\":448}],\"file\":\"lib/transfer.c\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d/-/blob/lib/transfer.c\"}]}"

= child;\\n4028: \\n4029:     if(parent->set.priority.children && exclusive) {\\n4030:       /* exclusive: move all existing children underneath the new child */\\n4031:       struct 85. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /workspace/implement_rfc9218.md << 'EOF'\n# RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Stream Priority Implementation Plan\n\n## Changes Required\n\n### 1. struct Curl_data_priority in lib/urldata.h (line 1244-1254)\nAdd fields after the existing weight field:\n- `unsigned char h3_urgency` (0-7, default 3)\n- `BIT(h3_incremental)` (boolean flag)\n\n###2. CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY in include/curl/curl.h\nAdd new option before CURLOPT_LASTENTRY:\n```c\nCURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 324)\n```\n\n### 3. lib/setopt.c\nAdd case statement for CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY that:\n- Parses string in format \"u=N[,i]\"\n- Sets h3_urgency (0-7)\n- Sets h3_incremental flag if 'i' is present\n\n### 4. src/tool_cfgable.h\nAdd to struct OperationConfig:\n- `char *h3_priority_str`\n- `unsigned char h3_priority_urgency`\n- `bool h3_priority_incremental`\n\n### 5. src/tool_getparam.c\nAdd CLI alias for \"--h3-priority\" that accepts a string argument\n\n### 6. src/tool_operate.c\nWire the CLI option to CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY\n\n## Test Expectations\nTests should verify:\n- Parsing of priority string format\n- Valid urgency values (0-7)\n- Incremental flag parsing\n- Default values when not set\nEOF\ncat /workspace/implement_rfc9218.md\n",
  "description": "Create RFC 9218 implementation plan documentation"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "# RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Stream Priority Implementation Plan\n\n## Changes Required\n\n### 1. struct Curl_data_priority in lib/urldata.h (line 1244-1254)\nAdd fields after the existing weight field:\n- `unsigned char h3_urgency` (0-7, default 3)\n- `BIT(h3_incremental)` (boolean flag)\n\n###2. CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY in include/curl/curl.h\nAdd new option before CURLOPT_LASTENTRY:\n```c\nCURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 324)\n```\n\n### 3. lib/setopt.c\nAdd case statement for CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY that:\n- Parses string in format \"u=N[,i]\"\n- Sets h3_urgency (0-7)\n- Sets h3_incremental flag if 'i' is present\n\n### 4. src/tool_cfgable.h\nAdd to struct OperationConfig:\n- `char *h3_priority_str`\n- `unsigned char h3_priority_urgency`\n- `bool h3_priority_incremental`\n\n### 5. src/tool_getparam.c\nAdd CLI alias for \"--h3-priority\" that accepts a string argument\n\n### 6. src/tool_operate.c\nWire the CLI option to CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY\n\n## Test Expectations\nTests should verify:\n- Parsing of priority string format\n- Valid urgency values (0-7)\n- Incremental flag parsing\n- Default values when not set"
}
86. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Fetch and modify curl source files for RFC 9218",
  "prompt": "I need to implement HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) in curl. Please:\n\n1. Read these files from github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d:\n   - include/curl/curl.h (full file, ~2300 lines)\n   - lib/urldata.h (full file)\n   - lib/setopt.c (full file)\n   - src/tool_cfgable.h (full file)\n   - src/tool_getparam.c (first 500 lines to see aliases structure)\n   - src/tool_operate.c (search for httpversion usage around line 1685)\n\n2. For each file, identify the exact changes needed:\n   - curl.h: Add CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY (option 324) before CURLOPT_LASTENTRY\n   - urldata.h: Add h3_urgency (0-7) and h3_incremental fields to struct Curl_data_priority\n   - setopt.c: Add case for CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY with string parsing (\"u=N[,i]\" format)\n   - tool_cfgable.h: Add h3_priority_str, h3_priority_urgency, h3_priority_incremental fields\n   - tool_getparam.c: Add \"--h3-priority\" alias\n   - tool_operate.c: Wire the option like httpversion is wired\n\n3. Just provide a summary of where each change goes and what the code should look like.",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "aa46990ff3ad4f68e",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document.\n\n## Summary: HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) Implementation Guide\n\nBased on my analysis of the curl repository (github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d), here's where each change needs to go and what the code should look like:\n\n---\n\n### 1. **include/curl/curl.h** (Lines 2210-2213)\n**Location:** Add before `CURLOPT_LASTENTRY`\n\n**Current code:**\n```c\n  /* set a specific client IP for HAProxy PROXY protocol header? */\n  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 323),\n\n  CURLOPT_LASTENTRY /* the last unused */\n} CURLoption;\n```\n\n**Required changes:**\n```c\n  /* set a specific client IP for HAProxy PROXY protocol header? */\n  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 323),\n\n  /* HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218): \"u=N[,i]\" format */\n  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 324),\n\n  CURLOPT_LASTENTRY /* the last unused */\n} CURLoption;\n```\n\n**Note:** This shifts the option number from 323 (HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP) to 324 (H3_STREAM_PRIORITY).\n\n---\n\n### 2. **lib/urldata.h** (Lines 1244-1254)\n**Location:** Modify the `struct Curl_data_priority` definition\n\n**Current code:**\n```c\nstruct Curl_data_priority {\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n  struct Curl_easy *parent;\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n#endif\n  int weight;\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  BIT(exclusive);\n#endif\n};\n```\n\n**Required changes:**\n```c\nstruct Curl_data_priority {\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n  struct Curl_easy *parent;\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n#endif\n  int weight;\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  BIT(exclusive);\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  unsigned char h3_urgency;   /* 0-7 urgency value from RFC 9218 */\n  BIT(h3_incremental);        /* incremental flag from RFC 9218 */\n#endif\n};\n```\n\n**Note:** These fields are added only when HTTP/3 is enabled, keeping the struct compact when HTTP/3 is disabled.\n\n---\n\n### 3. **lib/setopt.c** (After line 3013, near CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS)\n**Location:** Add new case before `CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO`\n\n**Current code:**\n```c\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS:\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E:\n  {\n    struct Curl_easy *dep = va_arg(param, struct Curl_easy *);\n    if(!dep || GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(dep)) {\n      return Curl_data_priority_add_child(dep, data,\n                                          option == CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO:\n```\n\n**Required changes - Add between STREAM_DEPENDS_E and CONNECT_TO:**\n```c\n  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY:\n  {\n    /* Parse RFC 9218 format: \"u=N[,i]\"\n       where N is urgency (0-7) and optional ,i sets incremental flag */\n    const char *priority_str = va_arg(param, char *);\n    unsigned char urgency = 3; /* default urgency */\n    int incremental = 0;\n    \n    if(priority_str) {\n      char *endptr;\n      long val;\n      \n      /* Parse urgency value (u=N) */\n      if(strncmp(priority_str, \"u=\", 2) == 0) {\n        val = strtol(priority_str + 2, &endptr, 10);\n        if(val >= 0 && val <= 7) {\n          urgency = (unsigned char)val;\n        } else {\n          return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n        }\n        \n        /* Check for optional incremental flag (,i) */\n        if(endptr && *endptr == ',') {\n          if(*(endptr + 1) == 'i' && \n             (*(endptr + 2) == '\\0' || isspace(*(endptr + 2)))) {\n            incremental = 1;\n          } else {\n            return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n          }\n        }\n      } else {\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n      }\n    }\n    \n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n    data->set.priority.h3_urgency = urgency;\n    data->set.priority.h3_incremental = incremental ? TRUE : FALSE;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n    break;\n  }\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. **src/tool_cfgable.h** (After line 166, near httpversion)\n**Location:** In `struct OperationConfig`, add after the `httpversion` field\n\n**Current code:**\n```c\n  char *request_target;\n  long httpversion;\n  bool http09_allowed;\n```\n\n**Required changes:**\n```c\n  char *request_target;\n  long httpversion;\n  bool http09_allowed;\n  char *h3_priority_str;        /* raw priority string from --h3-priority */\n  unsigned char h3_priority_urgency;  /* parsed urgency value (0-7) */\n  bool h3_priority_incremental; /* parsed incremental flag */\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. **src/tool_getparam.c** (In the `aliases[]` array, around line 363)\n**Location:** Add to the aliases array - suggested placement after http3-only option around line 219\n\n**Current code (around lines 217-220):**\n```c\n  {\\\"04\\\",  \\\"http3\\\",                   ARG_NONE},\n  {\\\"05\\\",  \\\"http3-only\\\",              ARG_NONE},\n  {\\\"09\\\",  \\\"http0.9\\\",                 ARG_BOOL},\n```\n\n**Required changes - Add after http3-only:**\n```c\n  {\\\"04\\\",  \\\"http3\\\",                   ARG_NONE},\n  {\\\"05\\\",  \\\"http3-only\\\",              ARG_NONE},\n  {\\\"0H\\\",  \\\"h3-priority\\\",             ARG_STRING},\n  {\\\"09\\\",  \\\"http0.9\\\",                 ARG_BOOL},\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. **src/tool_operate.c** (Lines 1685-1688)\n**Location:** Wire the H3 priority option like httpversion is wired, right after the HTTP version section\n\n**Current code:**\n```c\n          if(config->httpversion)\n            my_setopt_enum(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION, config->httpversion);\n          else if(feature_http2)\n            my_setopt_enum(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION, CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS);\n```\n\n**Required changes - Add right after HTTP version block:**\n```c\n          if(config->httpversion)\n            my_setopt_enum(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION, config->httpversion);\n          else if(feature_http2)\n            my_setopt_enum(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION, CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS);\n\n          /* HTTP/3 stream priority */\n          if(config->h3_priority_str)\n            my_setopt_str(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, config->h3_priority_str);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. **lib/easyoptions.c** (Line 376)\n**Location:** Update the debug check value\n\n**Current code:**\n```c\n  return ((CURLOPT_LASTENTRY%10000) != (323 + 1));\n```\n\n**Required changes:**\n```c\n  return ((CURLOPT_LASTENTRY%10000) != (324 + 1));\n```\n\nThis ensures the consistency check passes with the new option number.\n\n---\n\n## Summary of Changes by File\n\n| File | Line Range | Type | Description |\n|------|-----------|------|-------------|\n| include/curl/curl.h | 2211-2212 | Add | CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY option definition (option 324) |\n| lib/urldata.h | 1254-1258 | Add | h3_urgency and h3_incremental fields to struct Curl_data_priority |\n| lib/setopt.c | 3014-3054 | Add | Case handler for CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY with RFC 9218 parsing |\n| src/tool_cfgable.h | 168-170 | Add | Three new fields in struct OperationConfig for priority data |\n| src/tool_getparam.c | 219 | Add | \"--h3-priority\" alias with code \"0H\" to aliases array |\n| src/tool_operate.c | 1689-1691 | Add | Wire the H3 priority option to libcurl like httpversion |\n| lib/easyoptions.c | 376 | Modify | Update debug check from 323 to 324 |\n\nAll changes are designed to follow existing curl patterns and conventions, maintain backward compatibility, and properly handle both HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 scenarios through conditional compilation.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "I need to implement HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) in curl. Please:\n\n1. Read these files from github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d:\n   - include/curl/curl.h (full file, ~2300 lines)\n   - lib/urldata.h (full file)\n   - lib/setopt.c (full file)\n   - src/tool_cfgable.h (full file)\n   - src/tool_getparam.c (first 500 lines to see aliases structure)\n   - src/tool_operate.c (search for httpversion usage around line 1685)\n\n2. For each file, identify the exact changes needed:\n   - curl.h: Add CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY (option 324) before CURLOPT_LASTENTRY\n   - urldata.h: Add h3_urgency (0-7) and h3_incremental fields to struct Curl_data_priority\n   - setopt.c: Add case for CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY with string parsing (\"u=N[,i]\" format)\n   - tool_cfgable.h: Add h3_priority_str, h3_priority_urgency, h3_priority_incremental fields\n   - tool_getparam.c: Add \"--h3-priority\" alias\n   - tool_operate.c: Wire the option like httpversion is wired\n\n3. Just provide a summary of where each change goes and what the code should look like.",

  "totalDurationMs": 67368,
  "totalTokens": 57468,
  "totalToolUseCount": 32,

            /* alt-svc cache file name */\\n54:   char *hsts;               /* HSTS cache file name */\\n55:   bool cookiesession;       /* new session? */\\n56:   bool encoding;            /* Accept-Encoding please */\\n57:   bool tr_encoding;   /* dot notation: 1.1.1.1;2.2.2.2 */\\n89:   char *dns_interface; /* interface name */\\n90:   char *dns_ipv4_addr; /* dot notation */\\n91:   char *dns_ipv6_addr; /* dot notation */\\n92:   char *userpwd;\\n93:   char *login_options;\\n94:   char *tls_username;\\n95:   char *tls_password;\\n96:   char *tls_authtype;\\n97:   char *proxy_tls_username;\\n98:   char *proxy_tls_password;\\n99:   char *proxy_tls_authtype;\\n100:   char *proxyuserpwd;\\n101:   char *proxy;\\n102:   int proxyver;       /* Authorization identity (identity to use) */\\n109:   bool sasl_ir;             /* Enable/disable SASL initial response */\\n110:   bool proxytunnel;\\n111:   bool ftp_append;          /* APPE on ftp */\\n112:   bool use_ascii;           /* select ascii or text transfer */\\n113:   bool autoreferer;         /* automatically set referer */\\n114:   bool failonerror;         /* fail on (HTTP) errors */\\n115:   bool failwithbody;        /* fail on (HTTP) errors but still store body */\\n116:   bool show_headers;        /* show headers to data output */\\n117:   bool no_body;             /* don't get the body */\\n118:   bool dirlistonly;     /* set TRUE to allow insecure SSL connects\\n174:                                for DoH */\\n175:   bool proxy_insecure_ok;   /* set TRUE to allow insecure SSL connects\\n176:                                for proxy */\\n177:   bool terminal_binary_ok;\\n178:   bool verifystatus;\\n179:   bool doh_verifystatus;\\n180:   bool create_dirs;\\n181:   bool ftp_create_dirs;\\n182:   bool ftp_skip_ip;\\n183:   bool proxynegotiate;\\n184:   bool proxyntlm;\\n185:   bool proxydigest;\\n186:   bool proxybasic;\\n187:   bool proxyanyauth;\\n188:   bool jsoned; /* send to peer */\\n212:   curl_off_t recvpersecond; /* receive from peer */\\n213: \\n214:   bool ftp_ssl;\\n215:   bool ftp_ssl_reqd;\\n216:   bool ftp_ssl_control;\\n217:   bool ftp_ssl_ccc;\\n218:   int ftp_ssl_ccc_mode;\\n219:   char *preproxy;\\n220:   bool socks5_gssapi_nec; /* set authentication service name for HTTP and\\n224:                                SOCKS5 proxies */\\n225:   char *service_name;       /* set authentication service name for DIGEST-MD5,\\n226:                                Kerberos 5 and SPNEGO */\\n227: \\n228:   bool tcp_nodelay;\\n229:   bool tcp_fastopen;\\n230:   long req_retry;           /* number of retries */\\n231:   bool retry_all_errors;    /* retry on any error */\\n232:   bool retry_connrefused;   /* set connection refused as a transient error */\\n233:   long retry_delay;         /* delay between retries (in seconds) */\\n234:   long retry_maxtime;       /* maximum time to keep retrying */\\n235: \\n236:   char *ftp_account;        /* for ACCT */\\n237:   char *ftp_alternative_to_user;  /* send command if USER/PASS fails */\\n238:   int ftp_filemethod;\\n239:   long mime_options;        /* Mime option flags. */\\n240:   long tftp_blksize;        /* TFTP BLKSIZE option */\\n241:   bool tftp_no_options;     /* do not send TFTP options requests */\\n242:   bool ignorecl;            /* --ignore-content-length */\\n243:   bool disable_sessionid;\\n244: \\n245:   bool raw;\\n246:   bool post301;\\n247:   bool post302;\\n248:   bool post303;\\n249:   bool nokeepalive;         /* for keepalive needs */\\n250:   long alivetime;\\n251:   bool content_disposition; /* use Content-disposition filename */\\n252: \\n253:   int default_node_flags;   /* default flags to search for each 'node', which\\n254:                                is basically each given URL to transfer */\\n255: \\n256:   bool xattr;               /* store metadata in extended attributes */\\n257:   long gssapi_delegation;\\n258:   bool ssl_allow_beast;     /* allow this SSL vulnerability */\\n259:   bool proxy_ssl_allow_beast; /* allow this SSL vulnerability for proxy */\\n260:   bool ssl_no_revoke;       /* disable SSL certificate revocation checks */\\n261:   bool ssl_revoke_best_effort; /* ignore SSL revocation offline/missing\\n262:                                   revocation list errors */\\n263: \\n264:   bool native_ca_store;        /* use the native OS CA store */\\n265:   bool proxy_native_ca_store;  /* use the native OS CA store for proxy */\\n266:   bool ssl_auto_client_cert;   /* automatically locate and use a client\\n267:                                   certificate for authentication (Schannel) */\\n268:   bool proxy_ssl_auto_client_cert; /* proxy version of ssl_auto_client_cert */\\n269:   char *oauth_bearer;             /* OAuth 2.0 bearer token */\\n270:   bool noalpn;                    /* enable/disable TLS ALPN extension */\\n271:   char *unix_socket_path;         /* path to Unix domain socket */\\n272:   bool abstract_unix_socket;      /* path to an abstract Unix domain socket */\\n273:   bool falsestart;\\n274:   bool path_as_is;\\n275:   long expect100timeout_ms;\\n276:   bool suppress_connect_headers;  /* suppress proxy CONNECT response headers\\n277:                                      from user callbacks */\\n278:   bool synthetic_error;           /* if TRUE, this is tool-internal error */\\n279:   bool ssh_compression;           /* enable/disable SSH compression */\\n280:   long happy_eyeballs_timeout_ms; /* happy eyeballs timeout in milliseconds.\\n281:                                      0 is valid. default: CURL_HET_DEFAULT. */\\n282:   bool haproxy_protocol;          /* whether to send HAProxy protocol v1 */\\n283:   char *haproxy_clientip;         /* client IP for HAProxy protocol */\\n284:   bool disallow_username_in_url;  /* disallow usernames in URLs */\\n285:   char *aws_sigv4;\\n286:   enum {\\n287:     CLOBBER_DEFAULT, /* Provides compatibility with previous versions of curl,\\n288:                         by using the default behavior for -o, -O, and -J.\\n289:                         If those options would have overwritten files, like\\n290:                         -o and -O would, then overwrite them. In the case of\\n291:                         -J, this will not overwrite any files. */\\n292:     CLOBBER_NEVER, /* If the file exists, always fail */\\n293:     CLOBBER_ALWAYS /* If the file exists, always overwrite it */\\n294:   } file_clobber_mode;\\n295:   struct GlobalConfig *global;\\n296:   struct OperationConfig *prev;\\n297:   struct OperationConfig *next;   /* Always last in the struct */\\n298:   struct State state;             /* for create_transfer() */\\n299:   bool rm_partial;                /* on error, remove partially written output\\n300:                                      files */\\n301: };\\n302: \\n303: struct GlobalConfig {\\n304:   bool showerror;                 /* show errors when silent */\\n305:   bool silent;                    /* don't show messages, --silent given */\\n306:   bool noprogress;                /* don't show progress bar */\\n307:   bool isatty;                    /* Updated internally if output is a tty */\\n308:   char *trace_dump;               /* file to dump the network trace to */\\n309:   FILE *trace_stream;\\n310:   bool trace_fopened;\\n311:   trace tracetype;\\n312:   bool tracetime;                 /* include timestamp? */\\n313:   bool traceids;                  /* include xfer-/conn-id? */\\n314:   int progressmode;               /* CURL_PROGRESS_BAR / CURL_PROGRESS_STATS */\\n315:   char *libcurl;                  /* Output libcurl code to this file name */\\n316:   bool fail_early;                /* exit on first transfer error */\\n317:   bool styled_output;             /* enable fancy output style detection */\\n318:   long ms_per_transfer;           /* start next transfer after (at least) this\\n319:                                      many milliseconds */\\n320: #ifdef CURLDEBUG\\n321:   bool test_event_based;\\n322: #endif\\n323:   bool parallel;\\n324:   unsigned short parallel_max; /* MAX_PARALLEL is the maximum */\\n325:   bool parallel_connect;\\n326:   char *help_category;            /* The help category, if set */\\n327:   struct var *variables;\\n328:   struct OperationConfig *first;\\n329:   struct OperationConfig *current;\\n330:   struct OperationConfig *last;   /* Always last in the struct */\\n331: };\\n332: \\n333: void config_init(struct OperationConfig *config);\\n334: void config_free(struct OperationConfig *config);\\n335: \\n336: #endif /* HEADER_CURL_TOOL_CFGABLE_H */\\n337: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/src/tool_cfgable.h\"}"

/* use our own printf() functions */\\n30: #include \\\"curlx.h\\\"\\n31: \\n32: #include \\\"tool_binmode.h\\\"\\n33: #include \\\"tool_cfgable.h\\\"\\n34: #include \\\"tool_cb_prg.h\\\"\\n35: #include \\\"tool_filetime.h\\\"\\n36: #include \\\"tool_formparse.h\\\"\\n37: #include \\\"tool_getparam.h\\\"\\n38: #include \\\"tool_helpers.h\\\"\\n39: #include \\\"tool_libinfo.h\\\"\\n40: \\\"tool_stderr.h\\\"\\n46: #include \\\"var.h\\\"\\n47: \\n48: #include \\\"memdebug.h\\\" /* keep this as LAST include */\\n49: \\n50: #ifdef MSDOS\\n51: #  define USE_WATT32\\n52: #endif\\n53: \\n54: #define GetStr(str,val) do {                    \\\\\\n55:     if(*(str)) {                                \\\\\\n56:       free(*(str));                             \\\\\\n57:       *(str) = NULL;                            \\\\\\n58:     }                                           \\\\\\n59:     if((val)) {                                 \\\\\\n60:       *(str) = strdup((val));                   \\\\\\n61:       if(!(*(str))) {                           \\\\\\n62:         err = PARAM_NO_MEM;                     \\\\\\n63:         goto error;                             \\\\\\n64:       }                                         \\\\\\n65:     }                                           \\\\\\n66:   } while(0)\\n67: \\n68: struct LongShort {\\n69:   const char *letter; /* short name option */\\n70:   const char *lname;  /* long name option */\\n71:   enum {\\n72:     ARG_NONE,   /* stand-alone but not a boolean */\\n73:     ARG_BOOL,   /* accepts a --no-[name] prefix */\\n74:     ARG_STRING, /* requires an argument */\\n75:     ARG_FILENAME /* requires an argument, usually a file name */\\n76:   } desc;\\n77: };\\n78: \\n79: static const struct LongShort aliases[]= {\\n80:   /* 'letter' strings with more than one character have *no* short option to\\n81:      mention. */\\n82:   {\\\"*@\\\", \\\"url\\\",                      ARG_STRING},\\n83:   {\\\"*4\\\", \\\"dns-ipv4-addr\\\",            ARG_STRING},\\n84:   {\\\"*6\\\", \\\"dns-ipv6-addr\\\",            ARG_STRING},\\n85:   {\\\"*a\\\", \\\"random-file\\\",              ARG_FILENAME},\\n86:   {\\\"*b\\\", \\\"egd-file\\\",                 ARG_STRING},\\n87:   {\\\"*B\\\", \\\"oauth2-bearer\\\",            ARG_STRING},\\n88:   {\\\"*c\\\", \\\"connect-timeout\\\",          ARG_STRING},\\n89:   {\\\"*C\\\", \\\"doh-url\\\"        ,          ARG_STRING},\\n90:   {\\\"*d\\\", \\\"ciphers\\\",                  ARG_STRING},\\n91:   {\\\"*D\\\", \\\"dns-interface\\\",            ARG_STRING},\\n92:   {\\\"*e\\\", \\\"disable-epsv\\\",             ARG_BOOL},\\n93:   {\\\"*f\\\", \\\"disallow-username-in-url\\\", ARG_BOOL},\\n94:   {\\\"*E\\\", \\\"epsv\\\",                     ARG_BOOL},\\n95:          /* 'epsv' made like this to make --no-epsv and --epsv to work\\n96:              although --disable-epsv is the documented option */\\n97:   {\\\"*F\\\", \\\"dns-servers\\\",              ARG_STRING},\\n98:   {\\\"*g\\\", \\\"trace\\\",                    ARG_FILENAME},\\n99:   {\\\"*G\\\", \\\"npn\\\",                      ARG_BOOL},\\n100:   {\\\"*h\\\", \\\"trace-ascii\\\",              ARG_FILENAME},\\n101:   {\\\"*H\\\", \\\"alpn\\\",                     ARG_BOOL},\\n102:   {\\\"*i\\\", \\\"limit-rate\\\",               ARG_STRING},\\n103:   {\\\"*I\\\", \\\"rate\\\",                     ARG_STRING},\\n104:   {\\\"*j\\\", \\\"compressed\\\",               ARG_BOOL},\\n105:   {\\\"*J\\\", \\\"tr-encoding\\\", previous name */\\n128:   {\\\"*X\\\", \\\"haproxy-protocol\\\",         ARG_BOOL},\\n129:   {\\\"*P\\\", \\\"haproxy-clientip\\\",         ARG_STRING},\\n130:   {\\\"*y\\\", \\\"max-filesize\\\", \\\"ftp-ssl\\\",                  ARG_BOOL},\\n137:          /* 'ftp-ssl' deprecated name since 7.20.0 */\\n138:   {\\\"$a\\\", \\\"ssl\\\",                      ARG_BOOL},\\n139:          /* 'ssl' new option name in 7.20.0, previously this was ftp-ssl */\\n140:   {\\\"$b\\\", \\\"ftp-pasv\\\",                 ARG_BOOL},\\n141:   {\\\"$c\\\", \\\"socks5\\\",                   ARG_STRING},\\n142:                 ARG_STRING},\\n337:   {\\\"q\\\",  \\\"disable\\\",                  ARG_BOOL},\\n338:   {\\\"Q\\\",  \\\"quote\\\",                    ARG_STRING},\\n339:   {\\\"r\\\",  \\\"range\\\",                    ARG_STRING},\\n340:   {\\\"R\\\",  \\\"remote-time\\\",              ARG_BOOL},\\n341:   {\\\"s\\\",  \\\"silent\\\",                   ARG_BOOL},\\n342:   {\\\"S\\\",  \\\"show-error\\\",               ARG_BOOL},\\n343:   {\\\"t\\\",  \\\"telnet-option\\\",            ARG_STRING},\\n344:   {\\\"T\\\",  \\\"upload-file\\\",              ARG_FILENAME},\\n345:   {\\\"u\\\",  \\\"user\\\",                     ARG_STRING},\\n346:   {\\\"U\\\",  \\\"proxy-user\\\",               ARG_STRING},\\n347:   {\\\"v\\\",  \\\"verbose\\\",                  ARG_BOOL},\\n348:   {\\\"V\\\",  \\\"version\\\",                  ARG_BOOL},\\n349:   {\\\"w\\\",  \\\"write-out\\\",                ARG_STRING},\\n350:   {\\\"x\\\",  \\\"proxy\\\",                    ARG_STRING},\\n351:   {\\\"xa\\\", \\\"preproxy\\\",                 ARG_STRING},\\n352:   {\\\"X\\\",  \\\"request\\\",                  ARG_STRING},\\n353:   {\\\"Y\\\",  \\\"speed-limit\\\",              ARG_STRING},\\n354:   {\\\"y\\\",  \\\"speed-time\\\",               ARG_STRING},\\n355:   {\\\"z\\\",  \\\"time-cond\\\",                ARG_STRING},\\n356:   {\\\"Z\\\",  \\\"parallel\\\",                 ARG_BOOL},\\n357:   {\\\"Zb\\\", \\\"parallel-max\\\",             ARG_STRING},\\n358:   {\\\"Zc\\\", \\\"parallel-immediate\\\",       ARG_BOOL},\\n359:   {\\\"#\\\",  \\\"progress-bar\\\",             ARG_BOOL},\\n360:   {\\\"#m\\\", \\\"progress-meter\\\",           ARG_BOOL},\\n361:   {\\\":\\\",  \\\"next\\\",                     ARG_NONE},\\n362:   {\\\":a\\\", \\\"variable\\\",                 ARG_STRING},\\n363: };\\n364: \\n365: /* Split the argument of -E to 'certname' and 'passphrase' separated by colon.\\n366:  * We allow ':' and '\\\\' to be escaped by '\\\\' so that we can use certificate\\n367:  * nicknames containing ':'.  See <https://sourceforge.net/p/curl/bugs/1196/>\\n368:  * for details. */\\n369: #ifndef UNITTESTS\\n370: static\\n371: #endif\\n372: void parse_cert_parameter(const char *cert_parameter,\\n373:                           char **certname,\\n374:                           char **passphrase)\\n375: {\\n376:   size_t param_length = strlen(cert_parameter);\\n377:   size_t span;\\n378:   const char *param_place = NULL;\\n379:   char *certname_place = NULL;\\n380:   *certname = NULL;\\n381:   *passphrase = NULL;\\n382: \\n383:   /* most trivial assumption: cert_parameter is empty */\\n384:   if(param_length == 0)\\n385:     return;\\n386: \\n387:   /* next less trivial: cert_parameter starts 'pkcs11:' and thus\\n388:    * looks like a RFC7512 PKCS#11 URI which can be used as-is.\\n389:    * Also if cert_parameter contains no colon nor backslash, this\\n390:    * means no passphrase was given and no characters escaped */\\n391:   if(curl_strnequal(cert_parameter, \\\"pkcs11:\\\", 7) ||\\n392:      !strpbrk(cert_parameter, \\\":\\\\\\\\\\\")) {\\n393:     *certname = strdup(cert_parameter);\\n394:     return;\\n395:   }\\n396:   /* deal with escaped chars; find unescaped colon if it exists */\\n397:   certname_place = malloc(param_length + 1);\\n398:   if(!certname_place)\\n399:     return;\\n400: \\n401:   *certname = certname_place;\\n402:   param_place = cert_parameter;\\n403:   while(*param_place) {\\n404:     span = strcspn(param_place, \\\":\\\\\\\\\\\");\\n405:     strncpy(certname_place, param_place, span);\\n406:     param_place += span;\\n407:     certname_place += span;\\n408:     /* we just ate all the non-special chars. now we're on either a special\\n409:      * char or the end of the string. */\\n410:     switch(*param_place) {\\n411:     case '\\\\0':\\n412:       break;\\n413:     case '\\\\\\\\':\\n414:       param_place++;\\n415:       switch(*param_place) {\\n416:         case '\\\\0':\\n417:           *certname_place++ = '\\\\\\\\';\\n418:           break;\\n419:         case '\\\\\\\\':\\n420:           *certname_place++ = '\\\\\\\\';\\n421:           param_place++;\\n422:           break;\\n423:         case ':':\\n424:           *certname_place++ = ':';\\n425:           param_place++;\\n426:           break;\\n427:         default:\\n428:           *certname_place++ = '\\\\\\\\';\\n429:           *certname_place++ = *param_place;\\n430:           param_place++;\\n431:           break;\\n432:       }\\n433:       break;\\n434:     case ':':\\n435:       /* Since we live in a world of weirdness and confusion, the win32\\n436:          dudes can use : when using drive letters and thus c:\\\\file:password\\n437:          needs to work. In order not to break compatibility, we still use : as\\n438:          separator, but we try to detect when it is used for a file name! On\\n439:          windows. */\\n440: #ifdef WIN32\\n441:       if((param_place == &cert_parameter[1]) &&\\n442:          (cert_parameter[2] == '\\\\\\\\' || cert_parameter[2] == '/') &&\\n443:          (ISALPHA(cert_parameter[0])) ) {\\n444:         /* colon in the second column, followed by a backslash, and the\\n445:            first character is an alphabetic letter:\\n446: \\n447:            this is a drive letter colon */\\n448:         *certname_place++ = ':';\\n449:         param_place++;\\n450:         break;\\n451:       }\\n452: #endif\\n453:       /* escaped colons and Windows drive letter colons were handled\\n454:        * above; if we're still here, this is a separating colon */\\n455:       param_place++;\\n456:       if(*param_place) {\\n457:         *passphrase = strdup(param_place);\\n458:       }\\n459:       goto done;\\n460:     }\\n461:   }\\n462: done:\\n463:   *certname_place = '\\\\0';\\n464: }\\n465: \\n466: /* Replace (in-place) '%20' by '+' according to RFC1866 */\\n467: static size_t replace_url_encoded_space_by_plus(char *url)\\n468: {\\n469:   size_t orig_len = strlen(url);\\n470:   size_t orig_index = 0;\\n471:   size_t new_index = 0;\\n472: \\n473:   while(orig_index < orig_len) {\\n474:     if((url[orig_index] == '%') &&\\n475:        (url[orig_index + 1] == '2') &&\\n476:        (url[orig_index + 2] == '0')) {\\n477:       url[new_index] = '+';\\n478:       orig_index += 3;\\n479:     }\\n480:     else{\\n481:       if(new_index != orig_index) {\\n482:         url[new_index] = url[orig_index];\\n483:       }\\n484:       orig_index++;\\n485:     }\\n486:     new_index++;\\n487:   }\\n488: \\n489:   url[new_index] = 0; /* terminate string */\\n490: \\n491:   return new_index; /* new size */\\n492: }\\n493: \\n494: static void\\n495: GetFileAndPassword(char *nextarg, char **file, char **password)\\n496: {\\n497:   char *certname, *passphrase;\\n498:   parse_cert_parameter(nextarg, &certname, &passphrase);\\n499:   Curl_safefree(*file);\\n500:   *file = certname;\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/src/tool_getparam.c%3FL1-500\"}"

"{\"content\":\"897: \\n898:   case CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION:\\n899:     /*\\n900:      * This sets a requested HTTP version to be used. The value is one of\\n901:      * the listed enums in curl/curl.h.\\n902:      */\\n903:     arg = va_arg(param, long);\\n904:     switch(arg) {\\n905:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_NONE:\\n906: #ifdef USE_HTTP2\\n907:       /* TODO: this seems an undesirable quirk to force a behaviour on\\n908:        * lower implementations that they should recognize independently? */\\n909:       arg = CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS;\\n910: #endif\\n911:       /* accepted */\\n912:       break;\\n913:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_0:\\n914:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1:\\n915:       /* accepted */\\n916:       break;\\n917: #ifdef USE_HTTP2\\n918:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2_0:\\n919:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS:\\n920:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2_PRIOR_KNOWLEDGE:\\n921:       /* accepted */\\n922:       break;\\n923: #endif\\n924: #ifdef ENABLE_QUIC\\n925:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3:\\n926:     case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3ONLY:\\n927:       /* accepted */\\n928:       break;\\n929: #endif\\n930:     default:\\n931: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/setopt.c%3FL897-930\"}"

        /* CURLOPT_WRITEDATA */\\n1637:   void *in_set;      /* CURLOPT_READDATA */\\n1638:   void *writeheader; /* write the header to this if non-NULL */\\n1639:   unsigned short use_port; /* which port to use (when not using default) */\\n1640:   unsigned long httpauth;  /* if POST, set the fields' values here */\\n1646:   curl_seek_callback seek_func;      /* function that seeks the input */\\n1647:   curl_off_t postfieldsize; /* if POST, this might have a size to use instead\\n1648:                                of strlen(), and then the data *may* be binary\\n1649:                                (contain zero bytes) */\\n1650: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_BINDLOCAL\\n1651:   unsigned short localport; /* local port number to bind to */\\n1652:   unsigned short localportrange; /* number of additional port numbers to test\\n1653:                                     in case the 'localport' one can't be\\n1654:                                     bind()ed */\\n1655: #endif\\n1656:   curl_write_callback fwrite_func;   /* function that stores the output */\\n1657:   curl_write_callback fwrite_header; /* function that stores headers */\\n1658:   curl_write_callback fwrite_rtp;    /* function that stores interleaved RTP */\\n1659:   curl_read_callback fread_func_set; /* function that reads the input */\\n1660:   curl_progress_callback fprogress; /* OLD and deprecated progress callback  */\\n1661:   curl_xferinfo_callback fxferinfo; /* progress callback */\\n1662:   curl_debug_callback fdebug; /* pointer to pass to the socket options callback */\\n1666:   curl_opensocket_callback fopensocket; /* function for checking/translating\\n1667:                                            the address and opening the\\n1668:                                            socket */\\n1669:   void *opensocket_client;\\n1670:   curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* pre-initial request user data */\\n1675: \\n1676:   void *seek_client;    /* pointer to pass to the seek callback */\\n1677: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\\n1678:   struct curl_slist *cookielist; /* list of cookie files set by\\n1679:                                     curl_easy_setopt(COOKIEFILE) calls */\\n1680: #endif\\n1681: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/urldata.h%3FL1620-1680\"}"

119. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nImplement RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Stream Priority support in curl\nThis script reads source files from Sourcegraph and creates modified versions locally\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport sys\n\ndef read_file_from_sourcegraph_output(filename, content_lines):\n    \"\"\"Parse content from Sourcegraph file read output\"\"\"\n    return '\\n'.join(content_lines)\n\ndef modify_curl_h(content):\n    \"\"\"Add CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY to curl.h\"\"\"\n    # Find the line with CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP\n    lines = content.split('\\n')\n    new_lines = []\n\n    for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n        new_lines.append(line)\n        if 'CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP' in line and 'CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT' in line:\n            # Insert the new option after this line\n            new_lines.append('')\n            new_lines.append('  /* HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218): \"u=N[,i]\" format */')\n            new_lines.append('  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 324),')\n\n    return '\\n'.join(new_lines)\n\ndef modify_urldata_h(content):\n    \"\"\"Add h3_urgency and h3_incremental fields to struct Curl_data_priority\"\"\"\n    lines = content.split('\\n')\n    new_lines = []\n\n    for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n        new_lines.append(line)\n        # Find the closing of struct Curl_data_priority\n        if line.strip().startswith('BIT(exclusive);') and i > 0:\n            # Check if this is inside Curl_data_priority\n            if any('struct Curl_data_priority' in lines[j] for j in range(max(0, i-20), i)):\n                new_lines.append('#ifdef USE_HTTP3')\n                new_lines.append('  unsigned char h3_urgency;   /* 0-7 urgency value from RFC 9218 */')\n                new_lines.append('  BIT(h3_incremental);        /* incremental flag from RFC 9218 */')\n                new_lines.append('#endif')\n\n    return '\\n'.join(new_lines)\n\nprint(\"RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Stream Priority Implementation\")\nprint(\"=\" * 60)\nprint(\"\\nThis script will modify the following files:\")\nprint(\"1. include/curl/curl.h - Add CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY\")\nprint(\"2. lib/urldata.h - Add h3_urgency and h3_incremental fields\")\nprint(\"3. lib/setopt.c - Add option handler with RFC 9218 parsing\")\nprint(\"4. src/tool_cfgable.h - Add fields to struct OperationConfig\")\nprint(\"5. src/tool_getparam.c - Add --h3-priority CLI option\")\nprint(\"6. src/tool_operate.c - Wire the option to libcurl\")\nprint(\"7. lib/easyoptions.c - Update consistency check\")\nprint(\"\\nNote: This is a placeholder script.\")\nprint(\"The actual implementation will use sg_read_file and Edit tools.\")\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/implement_changes.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nImplement RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Stream Priority support in curl\nThis script reads source files from Sourcegraph and creates modified versions locally\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport sys\n\ndef read_file_from_sourcegraph_output(filename, content_lines):\n    \"\"\"Parse content from Sourcegraph file read output\"\"\"\n    return '\\n'.join(content_lines)\n\ndef modify_curl_h(content):\n    \"\"\"Add CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY to curl.h\"\"\"\n    # Find the line with CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP\n    lines = content.split('\\n')\n    new_lines = []\n\n    for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n        new_lines.append(line)\n        if 'CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP' in line and 'CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT' in line:\n            # Insert the new option after this line\n            new_lines.append('')\n            new_lines.append('  /* HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218): \"u=N[,i]\" format */')\n            new_lines.append('  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 324),')\n\n    return '\\n'.join(new_lines)\n\ndef modify_urldata_h(content):\n    \"\"\"Add h3_urgency and h3_incremental fields to struct Curl_data_priority\"\"\"\n    lines = content.split('\\n')\n    new_lines = []\n\n    for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n        new_lines.append(line)\n        # Find the closing of struct Curl_data_priority\n        if line.strip().startswith('BIT(exclusive);') and i > 0:\n            # Check if this is inside Curl_data_priority\n            if any('struct Curl_data_priority' in lines[j] for j in range(max(0, i-20), i)):\n                new_lines.append('#ifdef USE_HTTP3')\n                new_lines.append('  unsigned char h3_urgency;   /* 0-7 urgency value from RFC 9218 */')\n                new_lines.append('  BIT(h3_incremental);        /* incremental flag from RFC 9218 */')\n                new_lines.append('#endif')\n\n    return '\\n'.join(new_lines)\n\nprint(\"RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Stream Priority Implementation\")\nprint(\"=\" * 60)\nprint(\"\\nThis script will modify the following files:\")\nprint(\"1. include/curl/curl.h - Add CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY\")\nprint(\"2. lib/urldata.h - Add h3_urgency and h3_incremental fields\")\nprint(\"3. lib/setopt.c - Add option handler with RFC 9218 parsing\")\nprint(\"4. src/tool_cfgable.h - Add fields to struct OperationConfig\")\nprint(\"5. src/tool_getparam.c - Add --h3-priority CLI option\")\nprint(\"6. src/tool_operate.c - Wire the option to libcurl\")\nprint(\"7. lib/easyoptions.c - Update consistency check\")\nprint(\"\\nNote: This is a placeholder script.\")\nprint(\"The actual implementation will use sg_read_file and Edit tools.\")\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/implement_changes.py",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

}

The terms\\n14:  * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\\n15:  *\\n16:  * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\\n17:  * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\\n18:  * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\\n19:  *\\n20:  * This software is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\\n21:  * KIND, either express or implied.\\n22:  *\\n23:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\\n24:  *\\n25:  ***************************************************************************/\\n26: \\n27: /*\\n28:  * If you have libcurl problems, all docs and details are found here:\\n29:  *   https://curl.se/libcurl/\\n30:  */\\n31: \\n32: #ifdef CURL_NO_OLDIES\\n33: #define CURL_STRICTER\\n34: #endif\\n35: \\n36: /* Compile-time deprecation macros. */\\n37: #if defined(__GNUC__) &&                                                \\\\\\n38:   ((__GNUC__ > 12) || ((__GNUC__ == 12) && (__GNUC_MINOR__ >= 1 ))) &&  \\\\\\n39:   !defined(__INTEL_COMPILER) &&                                         \\\\\\n40:   !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DEPRECATION) && !defined(BUILDING_LIBCURL)\\n41: #define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)                       \\\\\\n42:   __attribute__((deprecated(\\\"since \\\" # version \\\". \\\" message)))\\n43: #define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements) \\\\\\n44:       _Pragma(\\\"GCC diagnostic push\\\") \\\\\\n45:       _Pragma(\\\"GCC diagnostic ignored \\\\\\\"-Wdeprecated-declarations\\\\\\\"\\\") \\\\\\n46:       statements \\\\\\n47:       _Pragma(\\\"GCC diagnostic pop\\\")\\n48: #else\\n49: #define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)\\n50: #define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements)     statements\\n51: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/include/curl/curl.h%3FL1-50\"}",

    "content": "1: #ifndef CURLINC_CURL_H\n2: #define CURLINC_CURL_H\n3: /***************************************************************************\n4:  *                                  _   _ ____  _\n5:  *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n6:  *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n7:  *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n8:  *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n9:  *\n10:  * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n11:  *\n12:  * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n13:  * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n14:  * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n15:  *\n16:  * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n17:  * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n18:  * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n19:  *\n20:  * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n21:  * KIND, either express or implied.\n22:  *\n23:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n24:  *\n25:  ***************************************************************************/\n26: \n27: /*\n28:  * If you have libcurl problems, all docs and details are found here:\n29:  *   https://curl.se/libcurl/\n30:  */\n31: \n32: #ifdef CURL_NO_OLDIES\n33: #define CURL_STRICTER\n34: #endif\n35: \n36: /* Compile-time deprecation macros. */\n37: #if defined(__GNUC__) &&                                                \\\n38:   ((__GNUC__ > 12) || ((__GNUC__ == 12) && (__GNUC_MINOR__ >= 1 ))) &&  \\\n39:   !defined(__INTEL_COMPILER) &&                                         \\\n40:   !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DEPRECATION) && !defined(BUILDING_LIBCURL)\n41: #define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)                       \\\n42:   __attribute__((deprecated(\"since \" # version \". \" message)))\n43: #define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements) \\\n44:       _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic push\") \\\n45:       _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic ignored \\\"-Wdeprecated-declarations\\\"\") \\\n46:       statements \\\n47:       _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic pop\")\n48: #else\n49: #define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)\n50: #define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements)     statements\n51: ",
