full buffer as could be sent in\\n207:    a write callback.\\n208: \\n209:    The size was 16KB for many years but was bumped to 64KB because it makes\\n210:    libcurl able to do significantly faster uploads in some circumstances. Even\\n211:    larger buffers can help further, but this is deemed a fair memory/speed\\n212:    compromise. */\\n213: #define UPLOADBUFFER_DEFAULT 65536\\n214: #define UPLOADBUFFER_MAX (2*1024*1024)\\n215: #define UPLOADBUFFER_MIN CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE\\n216: \\n217: #define CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER 0xc0dedbadU\\n218: #ifdef DEBUGBUILD\\n219: /* On a debug build, we want to fail hard on easy handles that\\n220:  * are not NULL, but no longer have the MAGIC touch. This gives\\n221:  * us early warning on things only discovered by valgrind otherwise. */\\n222: #define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\\\\n223:   (((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER))? TRUE: \\\\\\n224:   (DEBUGASSERT(!(x)), FALSE))\\n225: #else\\n226: #define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\\\\n227:   ((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER))\\n228: #endif\\n229: \\n230: #ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\\n231: /* Types needed for krb5-ftp connections */\\n232: struct krb5buffer {\\n233:   void *data;\\n234:   size_t size;\\n235:   size_t index;\\n236:   BIT(eof_flag);\\n237: };\\n238: \\n239: enum protection_level {\\n240:   PROT_NONE, /* first in list */\\n241:   PROT_CLEAR,\\n242:   PROT_SAFE,\\n243:   PROT_CONFIDENTIAL,\\n244:   PROT_PRIVATE,\\n245:   PROT_CMD,\\n246:   PROT_LAST /* last in list */\\n247: };\\n248: #endif\\n249: \\n250: /* enum for the nonblocking SSL connection state machine */\\n251: typedef enum {\\n252:   ssl_connect_1,\\n253:   ssl_connect_2,\\n254:   ssl_connect_2_reading,\\n255:   ssl_connect_2_writing,\\n256:   ssl_connect_3,\\n257:   ssl_connect_done\\n258: } ssl_connect_state;\\n259: \\n260: typedef enum {\\n261:   ssl_connection_none,\\n262:   ssl_connection_negotiating,\\n263:   ssl_connection_complete\\n264: } ssl_connection_state;\\n265: \\n266: /* SSL backend-specific data; declared differently by each SSL backend */\\n267: struct ssl_backend_data;\\n268: \\n269: struct ssl_primary_config {\\n270:   char *CApath;          /* certificate dir (doesn't work on windows) */\\n271:   char *CAfile;          /* certificate to verify peer against */\\n272:   char *issuercert;      /* optional issuer certificate filename */\\n273:   char *clientcert;\\n274:   char *cipher_list;     /* list of ciphers to use */\\n275:   char *cipher_list13;   /* list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites to use */\\n276:   char *pinned_key;\\n277:   char *CRLfile;         /* CRL to check certificate revocation */\\n278:   struct curl_blob *cert_blob;\\n279:   struct curl_blob *ca_info_blob;\\n280:   struct curl_blob *issuercert_blob;\\n281: #ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\\n282:   char *username; /* TLS username (for, e.g., SRP) */\\n283:   char *password; /* TLS password (for, e.g., SRP) */\\n284: #endif\\n285:   char *curves;          /* list of curves to use */\\n286:   unsigned char ssl_options;  /* the CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS bitmask */\\n287:   unsigned int version_max; /* max supported version the client wants to use */\\n288:   unsigned char version;    /* what version the client wants to use */\\n289:   BIT(verifypeer);       /* set TRUE if this is desired */\\n290:   BIT(verifyhost);       /* set TRUE if CN/SAN must match hostname */\\n291:   BIT(verifystatus);     /* set TRUE if certificate status must be checked */\\n292:   BIT(sessionid);        /* cache session IDs or not */\\n293: };\\n294: \\n295: struct ssl_config_data {\\n296:   struct ssl_primary_config primary;\\n297:   long certverifyresult; /* result from the certificate verification */\\n298:   curl_ssl_ctx_callback fsslctx; /* function to initialize ssl ctx */\\n299:   void *fsslctxp;        /* parameter for call back */\\n300:   char *cert_type; /* format for certificate (default: PEM)*/\\n301:   char *key; /* private key file name */\\n302:   struct curl_blob *key_blob;\\n303:   char *key_type; /* format for private key (default: PEM) */\\n304:   char *key_passwd; /* plain text private key password */\\n305:   BIT(certinfo);     /* gather lots of certificate info */\\n306:   BIT(falsestart);\\n307:   BIT(enable_beast); /* allow this flaw for interoperability's sake */\\n308:   BIT(no_revoke);    /* disable SSL certificate revocation checks */\\n309:   BIT(no_partialchain); /* don't accept partial certificate chains */\\n310:   BIT(revoke_best_effort); /* ignore SSL revocation offline/missing revocation\\n311:                               list errors */\\n312:   BIT(native_ca_store); /* use the native ca store of operating system */\\n313:   BIT(auto_client_cert);   /* automatically locate and use a client\\n314:                               certificate for authentication (Schannel) */\\n315: };\\n316: \\n317: struct ssl_general_config {\\n318:   size_t max_ssl_sessions; /* SSL session id cache size */\\n319:   int ca_cache_timeout;  /* Certificate store cache timeout (seconds) */\\n320: };\\n321: \\n322: /* information stored about one single SSL session */\\n323: struct Curl_ssl_session {\\n324:   char *name;       /* host name for which this ID was used */\\n325:   char *conn_to_host; /* host name for the connection (may be NULL) */\\n326:   const char *scheme; /* protocol scheme used */\\n327:   void *sessionid;  /* as returned from the SSL layer */\\n328:   size_t idsize;    /* if known, otherwise 0 */\\n329:   long age;         /* just a number, the higher the more recent */\\n330:   int remote_port;  /* remote port */\\n331:   int conn_to_port; /* remote port for the connection (may be -1) */\\n332:   struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config; /* setup for this session */\\n333: };\\n334: \\n335: #ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\\n336: #include \\\"curl_sspi.h\\\"\\n337: #endif\\n338: \\n339: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH\\n340: /* Struct used for Digest challenge-response authentication */\\n341: struct digestdata {\\n342: #if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\\n343:   BYTE *input_token;\\n344:   size_t input_token_len;\\n345:   CtxtHandle *http_context;\\n346:   /* copy of user/passwd used to make the identity for http_context.\\n347:      either may be NULL. */\\n348:   char *user;\\n349:   char *passwd;\\n350: #else\\n351:   char *nonce;\\n352:   char *cnonce;\\n353:   char *realm;\\n354:   char *opaque;\\n355:   char *qop;\\n356:   char *algorithm;\\n357:   int nc; /* nonce count */\\n358:   unsigned char algo;\\n359:   BIT(stale); /* set true for re-negotiation */\\n360:   BIT(userhash);\\n361: #endif\\n362: };\\n363: #endif\\n364: \\n365: typedef enum {\\n366:   NTLMSTATE_NONE,\\n367:   NTLMSTATE_TYPE1,\\n368:   NTLMSTATE_TYPE2,\\n369:   NTLMSTATE_TYPE3,\\n370:   NTLMSTATE_LAST\\n371: } curlntlm;\\n372: \\n373: typedef enum {\\n374:   GSS_AUTHNONE,\\n375:   GSS_AUTHRECV,\\n376:   GSS_AUTHSENT,\\n377:   GSS_AUTHDONE,\\n378:   GSS_AUTHSUCC\\n379: } curlnegotiate;\\n380: \\n381: /* Struct used for GSSAPI (Kerberos V5) authentication */\\n382: #if defined(USE_KERBEROS5)\\n383: struct kerberos5data {\\n384: #if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\\n385:   CredHandle *credentials;\\n386:   CtxtHandle *context;\\n387:   TCHAR *spn;\\n388:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\\n389:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\\n390:   size_t token_max;\\n391:   BYTE *output_token;\\n392: #else\\n393:   gss_ctx_id_t context;\\n394:   gss_name_t spn;\\n395: #endif\\n396: };\\n397: #endif\\n398: \\n399: /* Struct used for SCRAM-SHA-1 authentication */\\n400: #ifdef USE_GSASL\\n401: #include <gsasl.h>\\n402: struct gsasldata {\\n403:   Gsasl *ctx;\\n404:   Gsasl_session *client;\\n405: };\\n406: #endif\\n407: \\n408: /* Struct used for NTLM challenge-response authentication */\\n409: #if defined(USE_NTLM)\\n410: struct ntlmdata {\\n411: #ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\\n412: /* The sslContext is used for the Schannel bindings. The\\n413:  * api is available on the Windows 7 SDK and later.\\n414:  */\\n415: #ifdef SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS\\n416:   CtxtHandle *sslContext;\\n417: #endif\\n418:   CredHandle *credentials;\\n419:   CtxtHandle *context;\\n420:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\\n421:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\\n422:   size_t token_max;\\n423:   BYTE *output_token;\\n424:   BYTE *input_token;\\n425:   size_t input_token_len;\\n426:   TCHAR *spn;\\n427: #else\\n428:   unsigned int flags;\\n429:   unsigned char nonce[8];\\n430:   unsigned int target_info_len;\\n431:   void *target_info; /* TargetInfo received in the ntlm type-2 message */\\n432: \\n433: #if defined(NTLM_WB_ENABLED)\\n434:   /* used for communication with Samba's winbind daemon helper ntlm_auth */\\n435:   curl_socket_t ntlm_auth_hlpr_socket;\\n436:   pid_t ntlm_auth_hlpr_pid;\\n437:   char *challenge; /* The received base64 encoded ntlm type-2 message */\\n438:   char *response;  /* The generated base64 ntlm type-1/type-3 message */\\n439: #endif\\n440: #endif\\n441: };\\n442: #endif\\n443: \\n444: /* Struct used for Negotiate (SPNEGO) authentication */\\n445: #ifdef USE_SPNEGO\\n446: struct negotiatedata {\\n447: #ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\\n448:   OM_uint32 status;\\n449:   gss_ctx_id_t context;\\n450:   gss_name_t spn;\\n451:   gss_buffer_desc output_token;\\n452: #else\\n453: #ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\\n454: #ifdef SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS\\n455:   CtxtHandle *sslContext;\\n456: #endif\\n457:   DWORD status;\\n458:   CredHandle *credentials;\\n459:   CtxtHandle *context;\\n460:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\\n461:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\\n462:   TCHAR *spn;\\n463:   size_t token_max;\\n464:   BYTE *output_token;\\n465:   size_t output_token_length;\\n466: #endif\\n467: #endif\\n468:   BIT(noauthpersist);\\n469:   BIT(havenoauthpersist);\\n470:   BIT(havenegdata);\\n471:   BIT(havemultiplerequests);\\n472: };\\n473: #endif\\n474: \\n475: #ifdef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\\n476: #define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) 0\\n477: #else\\n478: #define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) x->bits.proxy\\n479: #endif\\n480: \\n481: /*\\n482:  * Boolean values that concerns this connection.\\n483:  */\\n484: struct ConnectBits {\\n485: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\\n486:   BIT(httpproxy);  /* if set, this transfer is done through an HTTP proxy */\\n487:   BIT(socksproxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a socks proxy */\\n488:   BIT(proxy_user_passwd); /* user+password for the proxy? */\\n489:   BIT(tunnel_proxy);  /* if CONNECT is used to \\\"tunnel\\\" through the proxy.\\n490:                          This is implicit when SSL-protocols are used through\\n491:                          proxies, but can also be enabled explicitly by\\n492:                          apps */\\n493:   BIT(proxy_connect_closed); /* TRUE if a proxy disconnected the connection\\n494:                                 in a CONNECT request with auth, so that\\n495:                                 libcurl should reconnect and continue. */\\n496:   BIT(proxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a proxy - any type */\\n497: #endif\\n498:   /* always modify bits.close with the connclose() and connkeep() macros! */\\n499:   BIT(close); /* if set, we close the connection after this request */\\n500:   BIT(reuse); /* if set, this is a reused connection */\\n501:   BIT(altused); /* this is an alt-svc \\\"redirect\\\" */\\n502:   BIT(conn_to_host); /* if set, this connection has a \\\"connect to host\\\"\\n503:                         that overrides the host in the URL */\\n504:   BIT(conn_to_port); /* if set, this connection has a \\\"connect to port\\\"\\n505:                         that overrides the port in the URL (remote port) */\\n506:   BIT(ipv6_ip); /* we communicate with a remote site specified with pure IPv6\\n507:                    IP address */\\n508:   BIT(ipv6);    /* we communicate with a site using an IPv6 address */\\n509:   BIT(do_more); /* this is set TRUE if the ->curl_do_more() function is\\n510:                    supposed to be called, after ->curl_do() */\\n511:   BIT(protoconnstart);/* the protocol layer has STARTED its operation after\\n512:                          the TCP layer connect */\\n513:   BIT(retry);         /* this connection is about to get closed and then\\n514:                          re-attempted at another connection. */\\n515:   BIT(authneg);       /* TRUE when the auth phase has started, which means\\n516:                          that we are creating a request with an auth header,\\n517:                          but it is not the final request in the auth\\n518:                          negotiation. */\\n519: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\\n520:   BIT(ftp_use_epsv);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPSV, but if we find out\\n521:                          EPSV doesn't work we disable it for the forthcoming\\n522:                          requests */\\n523:   BIT(ftp_use_eprt);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPRT, but if we find out\\n524:                          EPRT doesn't work we disable it for the forthcoming\\n525:                          requests */\\n526:   BIT(ftp_use_data_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the data connection */\\n527:   BIT(ftp_use_control_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the control connection */\\n528: #endif\\n529: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\\n530:   BIT(netrc);         /* name+password provided by netrc */\\n531: #endif\\n532:   BIT(bound); /* set true if bind() has already been done on this socket/\\n533:                  connection */\\n534:   BIT(multiplex); /* connection is multiplexed */\\n535:   BIT(tcp_fastopen); /* use TCP Fast Open */\\n536:   BIT(tls_enable_alpn); /* TLS ALPN extension? */\\n537: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\\n538:   BIT(doh);\\n539: #endif\\n540: #ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\\n541:   BIT(abstract_unix_socket);\\n542: #endif\\n543:   BIT(tls_upgraded);\\n544:   BIT(sock_accepted); /* TRUE if the SECONDARYSOCKET was created with\\n545:                          accept() */\\n546:   BIT(parallel_connect); /* set TRUE when a parallel connect attempt has\\n547:                             started (happy eyeballs) */\\n548: };\\n549: \\n550: struct hostname {\\n551:   char *rawalloc; /* allocated \\\"raw\\\" version of the name */\\n552:   char *encalloc; /* allocated IDN-encoded version of the name */\\n553:   char *name;     /* name to use internally, might be encoded, might be raw */\\n554:   const char *dispname; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\\n555: };\\n556: \\n557: /*\\n558:  * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\\n559:  */\\n560: \\n561: #define KEEP_NONE  0\\n562: #define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\\n563: #define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\\n564: #define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\\n565:                                  might still be data to read */\\n566: #define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\\n567:                                   might still be data to write */\\n568: #define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\\n569: #define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\\n570: \\n571: #define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\\n572: #define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\\n573: \\n574: #if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\\n575: #define USE_CURL_ASYNC\\n576: struct Curl_async {\\n577:   char *hostname;\\n578:   struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\\n579:   struct thread_data *tdata;\\n580:   void *resolver; /* resolver state, if it is used in the URL state -\\n581:                      ares_channel e.g. */\\n582:   int port;\\n583:   int status; /* if done is TRUE, this is the status from the callback */\\n584:   BIT(done);  /* set TRUE when the lookup is complete */\\n585: };\\n586: \\n587: #endif\\n588: \\n589: #define FIRSTSOCKET     0\\n590: #define SECONDARYSOCKET 1\\n591: \\n592: enum expect100 {\\n593:   EXP100_SEND_DATA,           /* enough waiting, just send the body now */\\n594:   EXP100_AWAITING_CONTINUE,   /* waiting for the 100 Continue header */\\n595:   EXP100_SENDING_REQUEST,     /* still sending the request but will wait for\\n596:                                  the 100 header once done with the request */\\n597:   EXP100_FAILED               /* used on 417 Expectation Failed */\\n598: };\\n599: \\n600: enum upgrade101 {\\n601:   UPGR101_INIT,               /* default state */\\n602:   UPGR101_WS,                 /* upgrade to WebSockets requested */\\n603:   UPGR101_H2,                 /* upgrade to HTTP/2 requested */\\n604:   UPGR101_RECEIVED,           /* 101 response received */\\n605:   UPGR101_WORKING             /* talking upgraded protocol */\\n606: };\\n607: \\n608: enum doh_slots {\\n609:   /* Explicit values for first two symbols so as to match hard-coded\\n610:    * constants in existing code\\n611:    */\\n612:   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V4 = 0, /* make 'V4' stand out for readability */\\n613:   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V6 = 1, /* 'V6' likewise */\\n614: \\n615:   /* Space here for (possibly build-specific) additional slot definitions */\\n616: \\n617:   /* for example */\\n618:   /* #ifdef WANT_DOH_FOOBAR_TXT */\\n619:   /*   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_FOOBAR_TXT, */\\n620:   /* #endif */\\n621: \\n622:   /* AFTER all slot definitions, establish how many we have */\\n623:   DOH_PROBE_SLOTS\\n624: };\\n625: \\n626: /*\\n627:  * Request specific data in the easy handle (Curl_easy).  Previously,\\n628:  * these members were on the connectdata struct but since a conn struct may\\n629:  * now be shared between different Curl_easys, we store connection-specific\\n630:  * data here. This struct only keeps stuff that's interesting for *this*\\n631:  * request, as it will be cleared between multiple ones\\n632:  */\\n633: struct SingleRequest {\\n634:   curl_off_t size;        /* -1 if unknown at this point */\\n635:   curl_off_t maxdownload; /* in bytes, the maximum amount of data to fetch,\\n636:                              -1 means unlimited */\\n637:   curl_off_t bytecount;         /* total number of bytes read */\\n638:   curl_off_t writebytecount;    /* number of bytes written */\\n639: \\n640:   curl_off_t pendingheader;      /* this many bytes left to send is actually\\n641:                                     header and not body */\\n642:   struct curltime start;         /* transfer started at this time */\\n643:   unsigned int headerbytecount;  /* received server headers (not CONNECT\\n644:                                     headers) */\\n645:   unsigned int allheadercount;   /* all received headers (server + CONNECT) */\\n646:   unsigned int deductheadercount; /* this amount of bytes doesn't count when\\n647:                                      we check if anything has been transferred\\n648:                                      at the end of a connection. We use this\\n649:                                      counter to make only a 100 reply (without\\n650:                                      a following second response code) result\\n651:                                      in a CURLE_GOT_NOTHING error code */\\n652:   enum {\\n653:     HEADER_NORMAL,              /* no bad header at all */\\n654:     HEADER_PARTHEADER,          /* part of the chunk is a bad header, the rest\\n655:                                    is normal data */\\n656:     HEADER_ALLBAD               /* all was believed to be header */\\n657:   } badheader;                  /* the header was deemed bad and will be\\n658:                                    written as body */\\n659:   int headerline;               /* counts header lines to better track the\\n660:                                    first one */\\n661:   char *str;                    /* within buf */\\n662:   curl_off_t offset;            /* possible resume offset read from the\\n663:                                    Content-Range: header */\\n664:   int httpcode;                 /* error code from the 'HTTP/1.? XXX' or\\n665:                                    'RTSP/1.? XXX' line */\\n666:   int keepon;\\n667:   struct curltime start100;      /* time stamp to wait for the 100 code from */\\n668:   enum expect100 exp100;        /* expect 100 continue state */\\n669:   enum upgrade101 upgr101;      /* 101 upgrade state */\\n670: \\n671:   /* Content unencoding stack. See sec 3.5, RFC2616. */\\n672:   struct contenc_writer *writer_stack;\\n673:   time_t timeofdoc;\\n674:   long bodywrites;\\n675:   char *location;   /* This points to an allocated version of the Location:\\n676:                        header data */\\n677:   char *newurl;     /* Set to the new URL to use when a redirect or a retry is\\n678:                        wanted */\\n679: \\n680:   /* 'upload_present' is used to keep a byte counter of how much data there is\\n681:      still left in the buffer, aimed for upload. */\\n682:   ssize_t upload_present;\\n683: \\n684:   /* 'upload_fromhere' is used as a read-pointer when we uploaded parts of a\\n685:      buffer, so the next read should read from where this pointer points to,\\n686:      and the 'upload_present' contains the number of bytes available at this\\n687:      position */\\n688:   char *upload_fromhere;\\n689: \\n690:   /* Allocated protocol-specific data. Each protocol handler makes sure this\\n691:      points to data it needs. */\\n692:   union {\\n693:     struct FILEPROTO *file;\\n694:     struct FTP *ftp;\\n695:     struct HTTP *http;\\n696:     struct IMAP *imap;\\n697:     struct ldapreqinfo *ldap;\\n698:     struct MQTT *mqtt;\\n699:     struct POP3 *pop3;\\n700:     struct RTSP *rtsp;\\n701:     struct smb_request *smb;\\n702:     struct SMTP *smtp;\\n703:     struct SSHPROTO *ssh;\\n704:     struct TELNET *telnet;\\n705:   } p;\\n706: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\\n707:   struct dohdata *doh; /* DoH specific data for this request */\\n708: #endif\\n709: #if defined(WIN32) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\\n710:   struct curltime last_sndbuf_update;  /* last time readwrite_upload called\\n711:                                           win_update_buffer_size */\\n712: #endif\\n713: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\\n714:   unsigned char setcookies;\\n715: #endif\\n716:   unsigned char writer_stack_depth; /* Unencoding stack depth. */\\n717:   BIT(header);        /* incoming data has HTTP header */\\n718:   BIT(content_range); /* set TRUE if Content-Range: was found */\\n719:   BIT(upload_done);   /* set to TRUE when doing chunked transfer-encoding\\n720:                          upload and we're uploading the last chunk */\\n721:   BIT(ignorebody);    /* we read a response-body but we ignore it! */\\n722:   BIT(http_bodyless); /* HTTP response status code is between 100 and 199,\\n723:                          204 or 304 */\\n724:   BIT(chunk);         /* if set, this is a chunked transfer-encoding */\\n725:   BIT(ignore_cl);     /* ignore content-length */\\n726:   BIT(upload_chunky); /* set TRUE if we are doing chunked transfer-encoding\\n727:                          on upload */\\n728:   BIT(getheader);    /* TRUE if header parsing is wanted */\\n729:   BIT(forbidchunk);  /* used only to explicitly forbid chunk-upload for\\n730:                         specific upload buffers. See readmoredata() in http.c\\n731:                         for details. */\\n732:   BIT(no_body);      /* the response has no body */\\n733: };\\n734: \\n735: /*\\n736:  * Specific protocol handler.\\n737:  */\\n738: \\n739: struct Curl_handler {\\n740:   const char *scheme;        /* URL scheme name. */\\n741: \\n742:   /* Complement to setup_connection_internals(). This is done before the\\n743:      transfer \\\"owns\\\" the connection. */\\n744:   CURLcode (*setup_connection)(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n745:                                struct connectdata *conn);\\n746: \\n747:   /* These two functions MUST be set to be protocol dependent */\\n748:   CURLcode (*do_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\\n749:   CURLcode (*done)(struct Curl_easy *, CURLcode, bool);\\n750: \\n751:   /* If the curl_do() function is better made in two halves, this\\n752:    * curl_do_more() function will be called afterwards, if set. For example\\n753:    * for doing the FTP stuff after the PASV/PORT command.\\n754:    */\\n755:   CURLcode (*do_more)(struct Curl_easy *, int *);\\n756: \\n757:   /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\\n758:    * after the connect() and everything is done, as a step in the connection.\\n759:    * The 'done' pointer points to a bool that should be set to TRUE if the\\n760:    * function completes before return. If it doesn't complete, the caller\\n761:    * should call the ->connecting() function until it is.\\n762:    */\\n763:   CURLcode (*connect_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\\n764: \\n765:   /* See above. */\\n766:   CURLcode (*connecting)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\\n767:   CURLcode (*doing)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\\n768: \\n769:   /* Called from the multi interface during the PROTOCONNECT phase, and it\\n770:      should then return a proper fd set */\\n771:   int (*proto_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n772:                        struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\\n773: \\n774:   /* Called from the multi interface during the DOING phase, and it should\\n775:      then return a proper fd set */\\n776:   int (*doing_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n777:                        struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\\n778: \\n779:   /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_MORE phase, and it should\\n780:      then return a proper fd set */\\n781:   int (*domore_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n782:                         struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\\n783: \\n784:   /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_DONE, PERFORM and\\n785:      WAITPERFORM phases, and it should then return a proper fd set. Not setting\\n786:      this will make libcurl use the generic default one. */\\n787:   int (*perform_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n788:                          struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\\n789: \\n790:   /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\\n791:    * by the curl_disconnect(), as a step in the disconnection.  If the handler\\n792:    * is called because the connection has been considered dead,\\n793:    * dead_connection is set to TRUE. The connection is (again) associated with\\n794:    * the transfer here.\\n795:    */\\n796:   CURLcode (*disconnect)(struct Curl_easy *, struct connectdata *,\\n797:                          bool dead_connection);\\n798: \\n799:   /* If used, this function gets called from transfer.c:readwrite_data() to\\n800:      allow the protocol to do extra reads/writes */\\n801:   CURLcode (*readwrite)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn,\\n802:                         ssize_t *nread, bool *readmore);\\n803: \\n804:   /* This function can perform various checks on the connection. See\\n805:      CONNCHECK_* for more information about the checks that can be performed,\\n806:      and CONNRESULT_* for the results that can be returned. */\\n807:   unsigned int (*connection_check)(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n808:                                    struct connectdata *conn,\\n809:                                    unsigned int checks_to_perform);\\n810: \\n811:   /* attach() attaches this transfer to this connection */\\n812:   void (*attach)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);\\n813: \\n814:   int defport;            /* Default port. */\\n815:   curl_prot_t protocol;  /* See CURLPROTO_* - this needs to be the single\\n816:                             specific protocol bit */\\n817:   curl_prot_t family;    /* single bit for protocol family; basically the\\n818:                             non-TLS name of the protocol this is */\\n819:   unsigned int flags;     /* Extra particular characteristics, see PROTOPT_* */\\n820: \\n821: };\\n822: \\n823: #define PROTOPT_NONE 0             /* nothing extra */\\n824: #define PROTOPT_SSL (1<<0)         /* uses SSL */\\n825: #define PROTOPT_DUAL (1<<1)        /* this protocol uses two connections */\\n826: #define PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION (1<<2) /* need action before socket close */\\n827: /* some protocols will have to call the underlying functions without regard to\\n828:    what exact state the socket signals. IE even if the socket says \\\"readable\\\",\\n829:    the send function might need to be called while uploading, or vice versa.\\n830: */\\n831: #define PROTOPT_DIRLOCK (1<<3)\\n832: #define PROTOPT_NONETWORK (1<<4)   /* protocol doesn't use the network! */\\n833: #define PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD (1<<5)    /* needs a password, and if none is set it\\n834:                                       gets a default */\\n835: #define PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY (1<<6)   /* protocol can't handle\\n836:                                         url query strings (?foo=bar) ! */\\n837: #define PROTOPT_CREDSPERREQUEST (1<<7) /* requires login credentials per\\n838:                                           request instead of per connection */\\n839: #define PROTOPT_ALPN (1<<8) /* set ALPN for this */\\n840: /* (1<<9) was PROTOPT_STREAM, now free */\\n841: #define PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS (1<<10) /* allow options part in the userinfo field\\n842:                                       of the URL */\\n843: #define PROTOPT_PROXY_AS_HTTP (1<<11) /* allow this non-HTTP scheme over a\\n844:                                          HTTP proxy as HTTP proxies may know\\n845:                                          this protocol and act as a gateway */\\n846: #define PROTOPT_WILDCARD (1<<12) /* protocol supports wildcard matching */\\n847: #define PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL (1<<13) /* Allow \\\"control bytes\\\" (< 32 ascii) in\\n848:                                        user name and password */\\n849: #define PROTOPT_NOTCPPROXY (1<<14) /* this protocol can't proxy over TCP */\\n850: \\n851: #define CONNCHECK_NONE 0                 /* No checks */\\n852: #define CONNCHECK_ISDEAD (1<<0)          /* Check if the connection is dead. */\\n853: #define CONNCHECK_KEEPALIVE (1<<1)       /* Perform any keepalive function. */\\n854: \\n855: #define CONNRESULT_NONE 0                /* No extra information. */\\n856: #define CONNRESULT_DEAD (1<<0)           /* The connection is dead. */\\n857: \\n858: struct proxy_info {\\n859:   struct hostname host;\\n860:   int port;\\n861:   unsigned char proxytype; /* curl_proxytype: what kind of proxy that is in\\n862:                               use */\\n863:   char *user;    /* proxy user name string, allocated */\\n864:   char *passwd;  /* proxy password string, allocated */\\n865: };\\n866: \\n867: struct ldapconninfo;\\n868: \\n869: #define TRNSPRT_TCP 3\\n870: #define TRNSPRT_UDP 4\\n871: #define TRNSPRT_QUIC 5\\n872: #define TRNSPRT_UNIX 6\\n873: \\n874: /*\\n875:  * The connectdata struct contains all fields and variables that should be\\n876:  * unique for an entire connection.\\n877:  */\\n878: struct connectdata {\\n879:   struct Curl_llist_element bundle_node; /* conncache */\\n880: \\n881:   /* chunk is for HTTP chunked encoding, but is in the general connectdata\\n882:      struct only because we can do just about any protocol through an HTTP\\n883:      proxy and an HTTP proxy may in fact respond using chunked encoding */\\n884:   struct Curl_chunker chunk;\\n885: \\n886:   curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* function closing the socket(s) */\\n887:   void *closesocket_client;\\n888: \\n889:   /* This is used by the connection cache logic. If this returns TRUE, this\\n890:      handle is still used by one or more easy handles and can only used by any\\n891:      other easy handle without careful consideration (== only for\\n892:      multiplexing) and it cannot be used by another multi handle! */\\n893: #define CONN_INUSE(c) ((c)->easyq.size)\\n894: \\n895:   /**** Fields set when inited and not modified again */\\n896:   curl_off_t connection_id; /* Contains a unique number to make it easier to\\n897:                                track the connections in the log output */\\n898: \\n899:   /* 'dns_entry' is the particular host we use. This points to an entry in the\\n900:      DNS cache and it will not get pruned while locked. It gets unlocked in\\n901:      multi_done(). This entry will be NULL if the connection is reused as then\\n902:      there is no name resolve done. */\\n903:   struct Curl_dns_entry *dns_entry;\\n904: \\n905:   /* 'remote_addr' is the particular IP we connected to. it is owned, set\\n906:    * and NULLed by the connected socket filter (if there is one). */\\n907:   const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *remote_addr;\\n908: \\n909:   struct hostname host;\\n910:   char *hostname_resolve; /* host name to resolve to address, allocated */\\n911:   char *secondaryhostname; /* secondary socket host name (ftp) */\\n912:   struct hostname conn_to_host; /* the host to connect to. valid only if\\n913:                                    bits.conn_to_host is set */\\n914: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\\n915:   struct proxy_info socks_proxy;\\n916:   struct proxy_info http_proxy;\\n917: #endif\\n918:   /* 'primary_ip' and 'primary_port' get filled with peer's numerical\\n919:      ip address and port number whenever an outgoing connection is\\n920:      *attempted* from the primary socket to a remote address. When more\\n921:      than one address is tried for a connection these will hold data\\n922:      for the last attempt. When the connection is actually established\\n923:      these are updated with data which comes directly from the socket. */\\n924: \\n925:   char primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\\n926:   char *user;    /* user name string, allocated */\\n927:   char *passwd;  /* password string, allocated */\\n928:   char *options; /* options string, allocated */\\n929:   char *sasl_authzid;     /* authorization identity string, allocated */\\n930:   char *oauth_bearer; /* OAUTH2 bearer, allocated */\\n931:   struct curltime now;     /* \\\"current\\\" time */\\n932:   struct curltime created; /* creation time */\\n933:   struct curltime lastused; /* when returned to the connection cache */\\n934:   curl_socket_t sock[2]; /* two sockets, the second is used for the data\\n935:                             transfer when doing FTP */\\n936:   Curl_recv *recv[2];\\n937:   Curl_send *send[2];\\n938:   struct Curl_cfilter *cfilter[2]; /* connection filters */\\n939: \\n940:   struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config;\\n941: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\\n942:   struct ssl_primary_config proxy_ssl_config;\\n943: #endif\\n944:   struct ConnectBits bits;    /* various state-flags for this connection */\\n945: \\n946:   const struct Curl_handler *handler; /* Connection's protocol handler */\\n947:   const struct Curl_handler *given;   /* The protocol first given */\\n948: \\n949:   /* Protocols can use a custom keepalive mechanism to keep connections alive.\\n950:      This allows those protocols to track the last time the keepalive mechanism\\n951:      was used on this connection. */\\n952:   struct curltime keepalive;\\n953: \\n954:   /**** curl_get() phase fields */\\n955: \\n956:   curl_socket_t sockfd;   /* socket to read from or CURL_SOCKET_BAD */\\n957:   curl_socket_t writesockfd; /* socket to write to, it may very\\n958:                                 well be the same we read from.\\n959:                                 CURL_SOCKET_BAD disables */\\n960: \\n961: #ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\\n962:   BIT(sec_complete); /* if Kerberos is enabled for this connection */\\n963:   unsigned char command_prot; /* enum protection_level */\\n964:   unsigned char data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\\n965:   unsigned char request_data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\\n966:   size_t buffer_size;\\n967:   struct krb5buffer in_buffer;\\n968:   void *app_data;\\n969:   const struct Curl_sec_client_mech *mech;\\n970:   struct sockaddr_in local_addr;\\n971: #endif\\n972: \\n973: #if defined(USE_KERBEROS5)    /* Consider moving some of the above GSS-API */\\n974:   struct kerberos5data krb5;  /* variables into the structure definition, */\\n975: #endif                        /* however, some of them are ftp specific. */\\n976: \\n977:   struct Curl_llist easyq;    /* List of easy handles using this connection */\\n978:   curl_seek_callback seek_func; /* function that seeks the input */\\n979:   void *seek_client;            /* pointer to pass to the seek() above */\\n980: \\n981:   /*************** Request - specific items ************/\\n982: #if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI) && defined(SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS)\\n983:   CtxtHandle *sslContext;\\n984: #endif\\n985: \\n986: #ifdef USE_GSASL\\n987:   struct gsasldata gsasl;\\n988: #endif\\n989: \\n990: #if defined(USE_NTLM)\\n991:   curlntlm http_ntlm_state;\\n992:   curlntlm proxy_ntlm_state;\\n993: \\n994:   struct ntlmdata ntlm;     /* NTLM differs from other authentication schemes\\n995:                                because it authenticates connections, not\\n996:                                single requests! */\\n997:   struct ntlmdata proxyntlm; /* NTLM data for proxy */\\n998: #endif\\n999: \\n1000: #ifdef USE_SPNEGO\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/curl--09e25b9d@38a2cef1e44568cc397af3ee85e19532e8f889a5/-/blob/lib/urldata.h%3FL1-1000\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: #ifndef HEADER_CURL_URLDATA_H\n2: #define HEADER_CURL_URLDATA_H\n3: /***************************************************************************\n4:  *                                  _   _ ____  _\n5:  *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n6:  *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n7:  *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n8:  *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n9:  *\n10:  * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n11:  *\n12:  * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n13:  * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n14:  * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n15:  *\n16:  * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n17:  * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n18:  * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n19:  *\n20:  * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n21:  * KIND, either express or implied.\n22:  *\n23:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n24:  *\n25:  ***************************************************************************/\n26: \n27: /* This file is for lib internal stuff */\n28: \n29: #include \"curl_setup.h\"\n30: \n31: #define PORT_FTP 21\n32: #define PORT_FTPS 990\n33: #define PORT_TELNET 23\n34: #define PORT_HTTP 80\n35: #define PORT_HTTPS 443\n36: #define PORT_DICT 2628\n37: #define PORT_LDAP 389\n38: #define PORT_LDAPS 636\n39: #define PORT_TFTP 69\n40: #define PORT_SSH 22\n41: #define PORT_IMAP 143\n42: #define PORT_IMAPS 993\n43: #define PORT_POP3 110\n44: #define PORT_POP3S 995\n45: #define PORT_SMB 445\n46: #define PORT_SMBS 445\n47: #define PORT_SMTP 25\n48: #define PORT_SMTPS 465 /* sometimes called SSMTP */\n49: #define PORT_RTSP 554\n50: #define PORT_RTMP 1935\n51: #define PORT_RTMPT PORT_HTTP\n52: #define PORT_RTMPS PORT_HTTPS\n53: #define PORT_GOPHER 70\n54: #define PORT_MQTT 1883\n55: \n56: #ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n57: /* CURLPROTO_GOPHERS (29) is the highest publicly used protocol bit number,\n58:  * the rest are internal information. If we use higher bits we only do this on\n59:  * platforms that have a >= 64 bit type and then we use such a type for the\n60:  * protocol fields in the protocol handler.\n61:  */\n62: #define CURLPROTO_WS     (1<<30)\n63: #define CURLPROTO_WSS    ((curl_prot_t)1<<31)\n64: #else\n65: #define CURLPROTO_WS 0\n66: #define CURLPROTO_WSS 0\n67: #endif\n68: \n69: /* This should be undefined once we need bit 32 or higher */\n70: #define PROTO_TYPE_SMALL\n71: \n72: #ifndef PROTO_TYPE_SMALL\n73: typedef curl_off_t curl_prot_t;\n74: #else\n75: typedef unsigned int curl_prot_t;\n76: #endif\n77: \n78: /* This mask is for all the old protocols that are provided and defined in the\n79:    public header and shall exclude protocols added since which are not exposed\n80:    in the API */\n81: #define CURLPROTO_MASK   (0x3ffffff)\n82: \n83: #define DICT_MATCH \"/MATCH:\"\n84: #define DICT_MATCH2 \"/M:\"\n85: #define DICT_MATCH3 \"/FIND:\"\n86: #define DICT_DEFINE \"/DEFINE:\"\n87: #define DICT_DEFINE2 \"/D:\"\n88: #define DICT_DEFINE3 \"/LOOKUP:\"\n89: \n90: #define CURL_DEFAULT_USER \"anonymous\"\n91: #define CURL_DEFAULT_PASSWORD \"ftp@example.com\"\n92: \n93: /* Convenience defines for checking protocols or their SSL based version. Each\n94:    protocol handler should only ever have a single CURLPROTO_ in its protocol\n95:    field. */\n96: #define PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP (CURLPROTO_HTTP|CURLPROTO_HTTPS|CURLPROTO_WS| \\\n97:                            CURLPROTO_WSS)\n98: #define PROTO_FAMILY_FTP  (CURLPROTO_FTP|CURLPROTO_FTPS)\n99: #define PROTO_FAMILY_POP3 (CURLPROTO_POP3|CURLPROTO_POP3S)\n100: #define PROTO_FAMILY_SMB  (CURLPROTO_SMB|CURLPROTO_SMBS)\n101: #define PROTO_FAMILY_SMTP (CURLPROTO_SMTP|CURLPROTO_SMTPS)\n102: #define PROTO_FAMILY_SSH  (CURLPROTO_SCP|CURLPROTO_SFTP)\n103: \n104: #if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH) ||   \\\n105:   !defined(CURL_DISABLE_POP3)\n106: /* these protocols support CURLOPT_DIRLISTONLY */\n107: #define CURL_LIST_ONLY_PROTOCOL 1\n108: #endif\n109: \n110: #define DEFAULT_CONNCACHE_SIZE 5\n111: \n112: /* length of longest IPv6 address string including the trailing null */\n113: #define MAX_IPADR_LEN sizeof(\"ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:255.255.255.255\")\n114: \n115: /* Default FTP/IMAP etc response timeout in milliseconds */\n116: #define RESP_TIMEOUT (120*1000)\n117: \n118: /* Max string input length is a precaution against abuse and to detect junk\n119:    input easier and better. */\n120: #define CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH 8000000\n121: \n122: \n123: #include \"cookie.h\"\n124: #include \"psl.h\"\n125: #include \"formdata.h\"\n126: \n127: #ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\n128: #include <netinet/in.h>\n129: #endif\n130: #ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN6_H\n131: #include <netinet/in6.h>\n132: #endif\n133: \n134: #include \"timeval.h\"\n135: \n136: #include <curl/curl.h>\n137: \n138: #include \"http_chunks.h\" /* for the structs and enum stuff */\n139: #include \"hostip.h\"\n140: #include \"hash.h\"\n141: #include \"splay.h\"\n142: #include \"dynbuf.h\"\n143: #include \"dynhds.h\"\n144: \n145: /* return the count of bytes sent, or -1 on error */\n146: typedef ssize_t (Curl_send)(struct Curl_easy *data,   /* transfer */\n147:                             int sockindex,            /* socketindex */\n148:                             const void *buf,          /* data to write */\n149:                             size_t len,               /* max amount to write */\n150:                             CURLcode *err);           /* error to return */\n151: \n152: /* return the count of bytes read, or -1 on error */\n153: typedef ssize_t (Curl_recv)(struct Curl_easy *data,   /* transfer */\n154:                             int sockindex,            /* socketindex */\n155:                             char *buf,                /* store data here */\n156:                             size_t len,               /* max amount to read */\n157:                             CURLcode *err);           /* error to return */\n158: \n159: #ifdef USE_HYPER\n160: typedef CURLcode (*Curl_datastream)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n161:                                     struct connectdata *conn,\n162:                                     int *didwhat,\n163:                                     bool *done,\n164:                                     int select_res);\n165: #endif\n166: \n167: #include \"mime.h\"\n168: #include \"imap.h\"\n169: #include \"pop3.h\"\n170: #include \"smtp.h\"\n171: #include \"ftp.h\"\n172: #include \"file.h\"\n173: #include \"vssh/ssh.h\"\n174: #include \"http.h\"\n175: #include \"rtsp.h\"\n176: #include \"smb.h\"\n177: #include \"mqtt.h\"\n178: #include \"ftplistparser.h\"\n179: #include \"multihandle.h\"\n180: #include \"c-hyper.h\"\n181: #include \"cf-socket.h\"\n182: \n183: #ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n184: # ifdef HAVE_GSSGNU\n185: #  include <gss.h>\n186: # elif defined HAVE_GSSAPI_GSSAPI_H\n187: #  include <gssapi/gssapi.h>\n188: # else\n189: #  include <gssapi.h>\n190: # endif\n191: # ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI_GSSAPI_GENERIC_H\n192: #  include <gssapi/gssapi_generic.h>\n193: # endif\n194: #endif\n195: \n196: #ifdef USE_LIBSSH2\n197: #include <libssh2.h>\n198: #include <libssh2_sftp.h>\n199: #endif /* USE_LIBSSH2 */\n200: \n201: #define READBUFFER_SIZE CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE\n202: #define READBUFFER_MAX  CURL_MAX_READ_SIZE\n203: #define READBUFFER_MIN  1024\n204: \n205: /* The default upload buffer size, should not be smaller than\n206:    CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE, as it needs to hold a full buffer as could be sent in\n207:    a write callback.\n208: \n209:    The size was 16KB for many years but was bumped to 64KB because it makes\n210:    libcurl able to do significantly faster uploads in some circumstances. Even\n211:    larger buffers can help further, but this is deemed a fair memory/speed\n212:    compromise. */\n213: #define UPLOADBUFFER_DEFAULT 65536\n214: #define UPLOADBUFFER_MAX (2*1024*1024)\n215: #define UPLOADBUFFER_MIN CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE\n216: \n217: #define CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER 0xc0dedbadU\n218: #ifdef DEBUGBUILD\n219: /* On a debug build, we want to fail hard on easy handles that\n220:  * are not NULL, but no longer have the MAGIC touch. This gives\n221:  * us early warning on things only discovered by valgrind otherwise. */\n222: #define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\n223:   (((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER))? TRUE: \\\n224:   (DEBUGASSERT(!(x)), FALSE))\n225: #else\n226: #define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\n227:   ((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER))\n228: #endif\n229: \n230: #ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n231: /* Types needed for krb5-ftp connections */\n232: struct krb5buffer {\n233:   void *data;\n234:   size_t size;\n235:   size_t index;\n236:   BIT(eof_flag);\n237: };\n238: \n239: enum protection_level {\n240:   PROT_NONE, /* first in list */\n241:   PROT_CLEAR,\n242:   PROT_SAFE,\n243:   PROT_CONFIDENTIAL,\n244:   PROT_PRIVATE,\n245:   PROT_CMD,\n246:   PROT_LAST /* last in list */\n247: };\n248: #endif\n249: \n250: /* enum for the nonblocking SSL connection state machine */\n251: typedef enum {\n252:   ssl_connect_1,\n253:   ssl_connect_2,\n254:   ssl_connect_2_reading,\n255:   ssl_connect_2_writing,\n256:   ssl_connect_3,\n257:   ssl_connect_done\n258: } ssl_connect_state;\n259: \n260: typedef enum {\n261:   ssl_connection_none,\n262:   ssl_connection_negotiating,\n263:   ssl_connection_complete\n264: } ssl_connection_state;\n265: \n266: /* SSL backend-specific data; declared differently by each SSL backend */\n267: struct ssl_backend_data;\n268: \n269: struct ssl_primary_config {\n270:   char *CApath;          /* certificate dir (doesn't work on windows) */\n271:   char *CAfile;          /* certificate to verify peer against */\n272:   char *issuercert;      /* optional issuer certificate filename */\n273:   char *clientcert;\n274:   char *cipher_list;     /* list of ciphers to use */\n275:   char *cipher_list13;   /* list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites to use */\n276:   char *pinned_key;\n277:   char *CRLfile;         /* CRL to check certificate revocation */\n278:   struct curl_blob *cert_blob;\n279:   struct curl_blob *ca_info_blob;\n280:   struct curl_blob *issuercert_blob;\n281: #ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n282:   char *username; /* TLS username (for, e.g., SRP) */\n283:   char *password; /* TLS password (for, e.g., SRP) */\n284: #endif\n285:   char *curves;          /* list of curves to use */\n286:   unsigned char ssl_options;  /* the CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS bitmask */\n287:   unsigned int version_max; /* max supported version the client wants to use */\n288:   unsigned char version;    /* what version the client wants to use */\n289:   BIT(verifypeer);       /* set TRUE if this is desired */\n290:   BIT(verifyhost);       /* set TRUE if CN/SAN must match hostname */\n291:   BIT(verifystatus);     /* set TRUE if certificate status must be checked */\n292:   BIT(sessionid);        /* cache session IDs or not */\n293: };\n294: \n295: struct ssl_config_data {\n296:   struct ssl_primary_config primary;\n297:   long certverifyresult; /* result from the certificate verification */\n298:   curl_ssl_ctx_callback fsslctx; /* function to initialize ssl ctx */\n299:   void *fsslctxp;        /* parameter for call back */\n300:   char *cert_type; /* format for certificate (default: PEM)*/\n301:   char *key; /* private key file name */\n302:   struct curl_blob *key_blob;\n303:   char *key_type; /* format for private key (default: PEM) */\n304:   char *key_passwd; /* plain text private key password */\n305:   BIT(certinfo);     /* gather lots of certificate info */\n306:   BIT(falsestart);\n307:   BIT(enable_beast); /* allow this flaw for interoperability's sake */\n308:   BIT(no_revoke);    /* disable SSL certificate revocation checks */\n309:   BIT(no_partialchain); /* don't accept partial certificate chains */\n310:   BIT(revoke_best_effort); /* ignore SSL revocation offline/missing revocation\n311:                               list errors */\n312:   BIT(native_ca_store); /* use the native ca store of operating system */\n313:   BIT(auto_client_cert);   /* automatically locate and use a client\n314:                               certificate for authentication (Schannel) */\n315: };\n316: \n317: struct ssl_general_config {\n318:   size_t max_ssl_sessions; /* SSL session id cache size */\n319:   int ca_cache_timeout;  /* Certificate store cache timeout (seconds) */\n320: };\n321: \n322: /* information stored about one single SSL session */\n323: struct Curl_ssl_session {\n324:   char *name;       /* host name for which this ID was used */\n325:   char *conn_to_host; /* host name for the connection (may be NULL) */\n326:   const char *scheme; /* protocol scheme used */\n327:   void *sessionid;  /* as returned from the SSL layer */\n328:   size_t idsize;    /* if known, otherwise 0 */\n329:   long age;         /* just a number, the higher the more recent */\n330:   int remote_port;  /* remote port */\n331:   int conn_to_port; /* remote port for the connection (may be -1) */\n332:   struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config; /* setup for this session */\n333: };\n334: \n335: #ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n336: #include \"curl_sspi.h\"\n337: #endif\n338: \n339: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH\n340: /* Struct used for Digest challenge-response authentication */\n341: struct digestdata {\n342: #if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n343:   BYTE *input_token;\n344:   size_t input_token_len;\n345:   CtxtHandle *http_context;\n346:   /* copy of user/passwd used to make the identity for http_context.\n347:      either may be NULL. */\n348:   char *user;\n349:   char *passwd;\n350: #else\n351:   char *nonce;\n352:   char *cnonce;\n353:   char *realm;\n354:   char *opaque;\n355:   char *qop;\n356:   char *algorithm;\n357:   int nc; /* nonce count */\n358:   unsigned char algo;\n359:   BIT(stale); /* set true for re-negotiation */\n360:   BIT(userhash);\n361: #endif\n362: };\n363: #endif\n364: \n365: typedef enum {\n366:   NTLMSTATE_NONE,\n367:   NTLMSTATE_TYPE1,\n368:   NTLMSTATE_TYPE2,\n369:   NTLMSTATE_TYPE3,\n370:   NTLMSTATE_LAST\n371: } curlntlm;\n372: \n373: typedef enum {\n374:   GSS_AUTHNONE,\n375:   GSS_AUTHRECV,\n376:   GSS_AUTHSENT,\n377:   GSS_AUTHDONE,\n378:   GSS_AUTHSUCC\n379: } curlnegotiate;\n380: \n381: /* Struct used for GSSAPI (Kerberos V5) authentication */\n382: #if defined(USE_KERBEROS5)\n383: struct kerberos5data {\n384: #if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n385:   CredHandle *credentials;\n386:   CtxtHandle *context;\n387:   TCHAR *spn;\n388:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\n389:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\n390:   size_t token_max;\n391:   BYTE *output_token;\n392: #else\n393:   gss_ctx_id_t context;\n394:   gss_name_t spn;\n395: #endif\n396: };\n397: #endif\n398: \n399: /* Struct used for SCRAM-SHA-1 authentication */\n400: #ifdef USE_GSASL\n401: #include <gsasl.h>\n402: struct gsasldata {\n403:   Gsasl *ctx;\n404:   Gsasl_session *client;\n405: };\n406: #endif\n407: \n408: /* Struct used for NTLM challenge-response authentication */\n409: #if defined(USE_NTLM)\n410: struct ntlmdata {\n411: #ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n412: /* The sslContext is used for the Schannel bindings. The\n413:  * api is available on the Windows 7 SDK and later.\n414:  */\n415: #ifdef SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS\n416:   CtxtHandle *sslContext;\n417: #endif\n418:   CredHandle *credentials;\n419:   CtxtHandle *context;\n420:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\n421:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\n422:   size_t token_max;\n423:   BYTE *output_token;\n424:   BYTE *input_token;\n425:   size_t input_token_len;\n426:   TCHAR *spn;\n427: #else\n428:   unsigned int flags;\n429:   unsigned char nonce[8];\n430:   unsigned int target_info_len;\n431:   void *target_info; /* TargetInfo received in the ntlm type-2 message */\n432: \n433: #if defined(NTLM_WB_ENABLED)\n434:   /* used for communication with Samba's winbind daemon helper ntlm_auth */\n435:   curl_socket_t ntlm_auth_hlpr_socket;\n436:   pid_t ntlm_auth_hlpr_pid;\n437:   char *challenge; /* The received base64 encoded ntlm type-2 message */\n438:   char *response;  /* The generated base64 ntlm type-1/type-3 message */\n439: #endif\n440: #endif\n441: };\n442: #endif\n443: \n444: /* Struct used for Negotiate (SPNEGO) authentication */\n445: #ifdef USE_SPNEGO\n446: struct negotiatedata {\n447: #ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n448:   OM_uint32 status;\n449:   gss_ctx_id_t context;\n450:   gss_name_t spn;\n451:   gss_buffer_desc output_token;\n452: #else\n453: #ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n454: #ifdef SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS\n455:   CtxtHandle *sslContext;\n456: #endif\n457:   DWORD status;\n458:   CredHandle *credentials;\n459:   CtxtHandle *context;\n460:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\n461:   SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\n462:   TCHAR *spn;\n463:   size_t token_max;\n464:   BYTE *output_token;\n465:   size_t output_token_length;\n466: #endif\n467: #endif\n468:   BIT(noauthpersist);\n469:   BIT(havenoauthpersist);\n470:   BIT(havenegdata);\n471:   BIT(havemultiplerequests);\n472: };\n473: #endif\n474: \n475: #ifdef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n476: #define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) 0\n477: #else\n478: #define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) x->bits.proxy\n479: #endif\n480: \n481: /*\n482:  * Boolean values that concerns this connection.\n483:  */\n484: struct ConnectBits {\n485: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n486:   BIT(httpproxy);  /* if set, this transfer is done through an HTTP proxy */\n487:   BIT(socksproxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a socks proxy */\n488:   BIT(proxy_user_passwd); /* user+password for the proxy? */\n489:   BIT(tunnel_proxy);  /* if CONNECT is used to \"tunnel\" through the proxy.\n490:                          This is implicit when SSL-protocols are used through\n491:                          proxies, but can also be enabled explicitly by\n492:                          apps */\n493:   BIT(proxy_connect_closed); /* TRUE if a proxy disconnected the connection\n494:                                 in a CONNECT request with auth, so that\n495:                                 libcurl should reconnect and continue. */\n496:   BIT(proxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a proxy - any type */\n497: #endif\n498:   /* always modify bits.close with the connclose() and connkeep() macros! */\n499:   BIT(close); /* if set, we close the connection after this request */\n500:   BIT(reuse); /* if set, this is a reused connection */\n501:   BIT(altused); /* this is an alt-svc \"redirect\" */\n502:   BIT(conn_to_host); /* if set, this connection has a \"connect to host\"\n503:                         that overrides the host in the URL */\n504:   BIT(conn_to_port); /* if set, this connection has a \"connect to port\"\n505:                         that overrides the port in the URL (remote port) */\n506:   BIT(ipv6_ip); /* we communicate with a remote site specified with pure IPv6\n507:                    IP address */\n508:   BIT(ipv6);    /* we communicate with a site using an IPv6 address */\n509:   BIT(do_more); /* this is set TRUE if the ->curl_do_more() function is\n510:                    supposed to be called, after ->curl_do() */\n511:   BIT(protoconnstart);/* the protocol layer has STARTED its operation after\n512:                          the TCP layer connect */\n513:   BIT(retry);         /* this connection is about to get closed and then\n514:                          re-attempted at another connection. */\n515:   BIT(authneg);       /* TRUE when the auth phase has started, which means\n516:                          that we are creating a request with an auth header,\n517:                          but it is not the final request in the auth\n518:                          negotiation. */\n519: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n520:   BIT(ftp_use_epsv);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPSV, but if we find out\n521:                          EPSV doesn't work we disable it for the forthcoming\n522:                          requests */\n523:   BIT(ftp_use_eprt);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPRT, but if we find out\n524:                          EPRT doesn't work we disable it for the forthcoming\n525:                          requests */\n526:   BIT(ftp_use_data_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the data connection */\n527:   BIT(ftp_use_control_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the control connection */\n528: #endif\n529: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n530:   BIT(netrc);         /* name+password provided by netrc */\n531: #endif\n532:   BIT(bound); /* set true if bind() has already been done on this socket/\n533:                  connection */\n534:   BIT(multiplex); /* connection is multiplexed */\n535:   BIT(tcp_fastopen); /* use TCP Fast Open */\n536:   BIT(tls_enable_alpn); /* TLS ALPN extension? */\n537: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n538:   BIT(doh);\n539: #endif\n540: #ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n541:   BIT(abstract_unix_socket);\n542: #endif\n543:   BIT(tls_upgraded);\n544:   BIT(sock_accepted); /* TRUE if the SECONDARYSOCKET was created with\n545:                          accept() */\n546:   BIT(parallel_connect); /* set TRUE when a parallel connect attempt has\n547:                             started (happy eyeballs) */\n548: };\n549: \n550: struct hostname {\n551:   char *rawalloc; /* allocated \"raw\" version of the name */\n552:   char *encalloc; /* allocated IDN-encoded version of the name */\n553:   char *name;     /* name to use internally, might be encoded, might be raw */\n554:   const char *dispname; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\n555: };\n556: \n557: /*\n558:  * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\n559:  */\n560: \n561: #define KEEP_NONE  0\n562: #define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\n563: #define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\n564: #define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\n565:                                  might still be data to read */\n566: #define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\n567:                                   might still be data to write */\n568: #define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\n569: #define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\n570: \n571: #define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\n572: #define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\n573: \n574: #if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\n575: #define USE_CURL_ASYNC\n576: struct Curl_async {\n577:   char *hostname;\n578:   struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\n579:   struct thread_data *tdata;\n580:   void *resolver; /* resolver state, if it is used in the URL state -\n581:                      ares_channel e.g. */\n582:   int port;\n583:   int status; /* if done is TRUE, this is the status from the callback */\n584:   BIT(done);  /* set TRUE when the lookup is complete */\n585: };\n586: \n587: #endif\n588: \n589: #define FIRSTSOCKET     0\n590: #define SECONDARYSOCKET 1\n591: \n592: enum expect100 {\n593:   EXP100_SEND_DATA,           /* enough waiting, just send the body now */\n594:   EXP100_AWAITING_CONTINUE,   /* waiting for the 100 Continue header */\n595:   EXP100_SENDING_REQUEST,     /* still sending the request but will wait for\n596:                                  the 100 header once done with the request */\n597:   EXP100_FAILED               /* used on 417 Expectation Failed */\n598: };\n599: \n600: enum upgrade101 {\n601:   UPGR101_INIT,               /* default state */\n602:   UPGR101_WS,                 /* upgrade to WebSockets requested */\n603:   UPGR101_H2,                 /* upgrade to HTTP/2 requested */\n604:   UPGR101_RECEIVED,           /* 101 response received */\n605:   UPGR101_WORKING             /* talking upgraded protocol */\n606: };\n607: \n608: enum doh_slots {\n609:   /* Explicit values for first two symbols so as to match hard-coded\n610:    * constants in existing code\n611:    */\n612:   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V4 = 0, /* make 'V4' stand out for readability */\n613:   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V6 = 1, /* 'V6' likewise */\n614: \n615:   /* Space here for (possibly build-specific) additional slot definitions */\n616: \n617:   /* for example */\n618:   /* #ifdef WANT_DOH_FOOBAR_TXT */\n619:   /*   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_FOOBAR_TXT, */\n620:   /* #endif */\n621: \n622:   /* AFTER all slot definitions, establish how many we have */\n623:   DOH_PROBE_SLOTS\n624: };\n625: \n626: /*\n627:  * Request specific data in the easy handle (Curl_easy).  Previously,\n628:  * these members were on the connectdata struct but since a conn struct may\n629:  * now be shared between different Curl_easys, we store connection-specific\n630:  * data here. This struct only keeps stuff that's interesting for *this*\n631:  * request, as it will be cleared between multiple ones\n632:  */\n633: struct SingleRequest {\n634:   curl_off_t size;        /* -1 if unknown at this point */\n635:   curl_off_t maxdownload; /* in bytes, the maximum amount of data to fetch,\n636:                              -1 means unlimited */\n637:   curl_off_t bytecount;         /* total number of bytes read */\n638:   curl_off_t writebytecount;    /* number of bytes written */\n639: \n640:   curl_off_t pendingheader;      /* this many bytes left to send is actually\n641:                                     header and not body */\n642:   struct curltime start;         /* transfer started at this time */\n643:   unsigned int headerbytecount;  /* received server headers (not CONNECT\n644:                                     headers) */\n645:   unsigned int allheadercount;   /* all received headers (server + CONNECT) */\n646:   unsigned int deductheadercount; /* this amount of bytes doesn't count when\n647:                                      we check if anything has been transferred\n648:                                      at the end of a connection. We use this\n649:                                      counter to make only a 100 reply (without\n650:                                      a following second response code) result\n651:                                      in a CURLE_GOT_NOTHING error code */\n652:   enum {\n653:     HEADER_NORMAL,              /* no bad header at all */\n654:     HEADER_PARTHEADER,          /* part of the chunk is a bad header, the rest\n655:                                    is normal data */\n656:     HEADER_ALLBAD               /* all was believed to be header */\n657:   } badheader;                  /* the header was deemed bad and will be\n658:                                    written as body */\n659:   int headerline;               /* counts header lines to better track the\n660:                                    first one */\n661:   char *str;                    /* within buf */\n662:   curl_off_t offset;            /* possible resume offset read from the\n663:                                    Content-Range: header */\n664:   int httpcode;                 /* error code from the 'HTTP/1.? XXX' or\n665:                                    'RTSP/1.? XXX' line */\n666:   int keepon;\n667:   struct curltime start100;      /* time stamp to wait for the 100 code from */\n668:   enum expect100 exp100;        /* expect 100 continue state */\n669:   enum upgrade101 upgr101;      /* 101 upgrade state */\n670: \n671:   /* Content unencoding stack. See sec 3.5, RFC2616. */\n672:   struct contenc_writer *writer_stack;\n673:   time_t timeofdoc;\n674:   long bodywrites;\n675:   char *location;   /* This points to an allocated version of the Location:\n676:                        header data */\n677:   char *newurl;     /* Set to the new URL to use when a redirect or a retry is\n678:                        wanted */\n679: \n680:   /* 'upload_present' is used to keep a byte counter of how much data there is\n681:      still left in the buffer, aimed for upload. */\n682:   ssize_t upload_present;\n683: \n684:   /* 'upload_fromhere' is used as a read-pointer when we uploaded parts of a\n685:      buffer, so the next read should read from where this pointer points to,\n686:      and the 'upload_present' contains the number of bytes available at this\n687:      position */\n688:   char *upload_fromhere;\n689: \n690:   /* Allocated protocol-specific data. Each protocol handler makes sure this\n691:      points to data it needs. */\n692:   union {\n693:     struct FILEPROTO *file;\n694:     struct FTP *ftp;\n695:     struct HTTP *http;\n696:     struct IMAP *imap;\n697:     struct ldapreqinfo *ldap;\n698:     struct MQTT *mqtt;\n699:     struct POP3 *pop3;\n700:     struct RTSP *rtsp;\n701:     struct smb_request *smb;\n702:     struct SMTP *smtp;\n703:     struct SSHPROTO *ssh;\n704:     struct TELNET *telnet;\n705:   } p;\n706: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n707:   struct dohdata *doh; /* DoH specific data for this request */\n708: #endif\n709: #if defined(WIN32) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\n710:   struct curltime last_sndbuf_update;  /* last time readwrite_upload called\n711:                                           win_update_buffer_size */\n712: #endif\n713: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n714:   unsigned char setcookies;\n715: #endif\n716:   unsigned char writer_stack_depth; /* Unencoding stack depth. */\n717:   BIT(header);        /* incoming data has HTTP header */\n718:   BIT(content_range); /* set TRUE if Content-Range: was found */\n719:   BIT(upload_done);   /* set to TRUE when doing chunked transfer-encoding\n720:                          upload and we're uploading the last chunk */\n721:   BIT(ignorebody);    /* we read a response-body but we ignore it! */\n722:   BIT(http_bodyless); /* HTTP response status code is between 100 and 199,\n723:                          204 or 304 */\n724:   BIT(chunk);         /* if set, this is a chunked transfer-encoding */\n725:   BIT(ignore_cl);     /* ignore content-length */\n726:   BIT(upload_chunky); /* set TRUE if we are doing chunked transfer-encoding\n727:                          on upload */\n728:   BIT(getheader);    /* TRUE if header parsing is wanted */\n729:   BIT(forbidchunk);  /* used only to explicitly forbid chunk-upload for\n730:                         specific upload buffers. See readmoredata() in http.c\n731:                         for details. */\n732:   BIT(no_body);      /* the response has no body */\n733: };\n734: \n735: /*\n736:  * Specific protocol handler.\n737:  */\n738: \n739: struct Curl_handler {\n740:   const char *scheme;        /* URL scheme name. */\n741: \n742:   /* Complement to setup_connection_internals(). This is done before the\n743:      transfer \"owns\" the connection. */\n744:   CURLcode (*setup_connection)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n745:                                struct connectdata *conn);\n746: \n747:   /* These two functions MUST be set to be protocol dependent */\n748:   CURLcode (*do_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n749:   CURLcode (*done)(struct Curl_easy *, CURLcode, bool);\n750: \n751:   /* If the curl_do() function is better made in two halves, this\n752:    * curl_do_more() function will be called afterwards, if set. For example\n753:    * for doing the FTP stuff after the PASV/PORT command.\n754:    */\n755:   CURLcode (*do_more)(struct Curl_easy *, int *);\n756: \n757:   /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n758:    * after the connect() and everything is done, as a step in the connection.\n759:    * The 'done' pointer points to a bool that should be set to TRUE if the\n760:    * function completes before return. If it doesn't complete, the caller\n761:    * should call the ->connecting() function until it is.\n762:    */\n763:   CURLcode (*connect_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n764: \n765:   /* See above. */\n766:   CURLcode (*connecting)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n767:   CURLcode (*doing)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n768: \n769:   /* Called from the multi interface during the PROTOCONNECT phase, and it\n770:      should then return a proper fd set */\n771:   int (*proto_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n772:                        struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n773: \n774:   /* Called from the multi interface during the DOING phase, and it should\n775:      then return a proper fd set */\n776:   int (*doing_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n777:                        struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n778: \n779:   /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_MORE phase, and it should\n780:      then return a proper fd set */\n781:   int (*domore_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n782:                         struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n783: \n784:   /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_DONE, PERFORM and\n785:      WAITPERFORM phases, and it should then return a proper fd set. Not setting\n786:      this will make libcurl use the generic default one. */\n787:   int (*perform_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n788:                          struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n789: \n790:   /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n791:    * by the curl_disconnect(), as a step in the disconnection.  If the handler\n792:    * is called because the connection has been considered dead,\n793:    * dead_connection is set to TRUE. The connection is (again) associated with\n794:    * the transfer here.\n795:    */\n796:   CURLcode (*disconnect)(struct Curl_easy *, struct connectdata *,\n797:                          bool dead_connection);\n798: \n799:   /* If used, this function gets called from transfer.c:readwrite_data() to\n800:      allow the protocol to do extra reads/writes */\n801:   CURLcode (*readwrite)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn,\n802:                         ssize_t *nread, bool *readmore);\n803: \n804:   /* This function can perform various checks on the connection. See\n805:      CONNCHECK_* for more information about the checks that can be performed,\n806:      and CONNRESULT_* for the results that can be returned. */\n807:   unsigned int (*connection_check)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n808:                                    struct connectdata *conn,\n809:                                    unsigned int checks_to_perform);\n810: \n811:   /* attach() attaches this transfer to this connection */\n812:   void (*attach)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);\n813: \n814:   int defport;            /* Default port. */\n815:   curl_prot_t protocol;  /* See CURLPROTO_* - this needs to be the single\n816:                             specific protocol bit */\n817:   curl_prot_t family;    /* single bit for protocol family; basically the\n818:                             non-TLS name of the protocol this is */\n819:   unsigned int flags;     /* Extra particular characteristics, see PROTOPT_* */\n820: \n821: };\n822: \n823: #define PROTOPT_NONE 0             /* nothing extra */\n824: #define PROTOPT_SSL (1<<0)         /* uses SSL */\n825: #define PROTOPT_DUAL (1<<1)        /* this protocol uses two connections */\n826: #define PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION (1<<2) /* need action before socket close */\n827: /* some protocols will have to call the underlying functions without regard to\n828:    what exact state the socket signals. IE even if the socket says \"readable\",\n829:    the send function might need to be called while uploading, or vice versa.\n830: */\n831: #define PROTOPT_DIRLOCK (1<<3)\n832: #define PROTOPT_NONETWORK (1<<4)   /* protocol doesn't use the network! */\n833: #define PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD (1<<5)    /* needs a password, and if none is set it\n834:                                       gets a default */\n835: #define PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY (1<<6)   /* protocol can't handle\n836:                                         url query strings (?foo=bar) ! */\n837: #define PROTOPT_CREDSPERREQUEST (1<<7) /* requires login credentials per\n838:                                           request instead of per connection */\n839: #define PROTOPT_ALPN (1<<8) /* set ALPN for this */\n840: /* (1<<9) was PROTOPT_STREAM, now free */\n841: #define PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS (1<<10) /* allow options part in the userinfo field\n842:                                       of the URL */\n843: #define PROTOPT_PROXY_AS_HTTP (1<<11) /* allow this non-HTTP scheme over a\n844:                                          HTTP proxy as HTTP proxies may know\n845:                                          this protocol and act as a gateway */\n846: #define PROTOPT_WILDCARD (1<<12) /* protocol supports wildcard matching */\n847: #define PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL (1<<13) /* Allow \"control bytes\" (< 32 ascii) in\n848:                                        user name and password */\n849: #define PROTOPT_NOTCPPROXY (1<<14) /* this protocol can't proxy over TCP */\n850: \n851: #define CONNCHECK_NONE 0                 /* No checks */\n852: #define CONNCHECK_ISDEAD (1<<0)          /* Check if the connection is dead. */\n853: #define CONNCHECK_KEEPALIVE (1<<1)       /* Perform any keepalive function. */\n854: \n855: #define CONNRESULT_NONE 0                /* No extra information. */\n856: #define CONNRESULT_DEAD (1<<0)           /* The connection is dead. */\n857: \n858: struct proxy_info {\n859:   struct hostname host;\n860:   int port;\n861:   unsigned char proxytype; /* curl_proxytype: what kind of proxy that is in\n862:                               use */\n863:   char *user;    /* proxy user name string, allocated */\n864:   char *passwd;  /* proxy password string, allocated */\n865: };\n866: \n867: struct ldapconninfo;\n868: \n869: #define TRNSPRT_TCP 3\n870: #define TRNSPRT_UDP 4\n871: #define TRNSPRT_QUIC 5\n872: #define TRNSPRT_UNIX 6\n873: \n874: /*\n875:  * The connectdata struct contains all fields and variables that should be\n876:  * unique for an entire connection.\n877:  */\n878: struct connectdata {\n879:   struct Curl_llist_element bundle_node; /* conncache */\n880: \n881:   /* chunk is for HTTP chunked encoding, but is in the general connectdata\n882:      struct only because we can do just about any protocol through an HTTP\n883:      proxy and an HTTP proxy may in fact respond using chunked encoding */\n884:   struct Curl_chunker chunk;\n885: \n886:   curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* function closing the socket(s) */\n887:   void *closesocket_client;\n888: \n889:   /* This is used by the connection cache logic. If this returns TRUE, this\n890:      handle is still used by one or more easy handles and can only used by any\n891:      other easy handle without careful consideration (== only for\n892:      multiplexing) and it cannot be used by another multi handle! */\n893: #define CONN_INUSE(c) ((c)->easyq.size)\n894: \n895:   /**** Fields set when inited and not modified again */\n896:   curl_off_t connection_id; /* Contains a unique number to make it easier to\n897:                                track the connections in the log output */\n898: \n899:   /* 'dns_entry' is the particular host we use. This points to an entry in the\n900:      DNS cache and it will not get pruned while locked. It gets unlocked in\n901:      multi_done(). This entry will be NULL if the connection is reused as then\n902:      there is no name resolve done. */\n903:   struct Curl_dns_entry *dns_entry;\n904: \n905:   /* 'remote_addr' is the particular IP we connected to. it is owned, set\n906:    * and NULLed by the connected socket filter (if there is one). */\n907:   const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *remote_addr;\n908: \n909:   struct hostname host;\n910:   char *hostname_resolve; /* host name to resolve to address, allocated */\n911:   char *secondaryhostname; /* secondary socket host name (ftp) */\n912:   struct hostname conn_to_host; /* the host to connect to. valid only if\n913:                                    bits.conn_to_host is set */\n914: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n915:   struct proxy_info socks_proxy;\n916:   struct proxy_info http_proxy;\n917: #endif\n918:   /* 'primary_ip' and 'primary_port' get filled with peer's numerical\n919:      ip address and port number whenever an outgoing connection is\n920:      *attempted* from the primary socket to a remote address. When more\n921:      than one address is tried for a connection these will hold data\n922:      for the last attempt. When the connection is actually established\n923:      these are updated with data which comes directly from the socket. */\n924: \n925:   char primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n926:   char *user;    /* user name string, allocated */\n927:   char *passwd;  /* password string, allocated */\n928:   char *options; /* options string, allocated */\n929:   char *sasl_authzid;     /* authorization identity string, allocated */\n930:   char *oauth_bearer; /* OAUTH2 bearer, allocated */\n931:   struct curltime now;     /* \"current\" time */\n932:   struct curltime created; /* creation time */\n933:   struct curltime lastused; /* when returned to the connection cache */\n934:   curl_socket_t sock[2]; /* two sockets, the second is used for the data\n935:                             transfer when doing FTP */\n936:   Curl_recv *recv[2];\n937:   Curl_send *send[2];\n938:   struct Curl_cfilter *cfilter[2]; /* connection filters */\n939: \n940:   struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config;\n941: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n942:   struct ssl_primary_config proxy_ssl_config;\n943: #endif\n944:   struct ConnectBits bits;    /* various state-flags for this connection */\n945: \n946:   const struct Curl_handler *handler; /* Connection's protocol handler */\n947:   const struct Curl_handler *given;   /* The protocol first given */\n948: \n949:   /* Protocols can use a custom keepalive mechanism to keep connections alive.\n950:      This allows those protocols to track the last time the keepalive mechanism\n951:      was used on this connection. */\n952:   struct curltime keepalive;\n953: \n954:   /**** curl_get() phase fields */\n955: \n956:   curl_socket_t sockfd;   /* socket to read from or CURL_SOCKET_BAD */\n957:   curl_socket_t writesockfd; /* socket to write to, it may very\n958:                                 well be the same we read from.\n959:                                 CURL_SOCKET_BAD disables */\n960: \n961: #ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n962:   BIT(sec_complete);
